We pay only for executed frames.\n* ✅ Horizontal scale is automatic—each step is naturally parallel across tenants.\n* ✅ Audit compliance: every state mutation is a DynamoDB record & EventBridge event.\n* ✅ Subsystems can be deployed independently (physics version `v2` can coexist with AI version `v5`).\n\nNegative / Trade-offs:\n\n* ❌ Cold-start latency. Mitigated via **provisioned concurrency** during peak hours.\n* ❌ 900 ms hard limit for a micro-tick. Complex scenes must split over multiple ticks.\n* ❌ Step Functions execution history impacts cost; we truncate history via `TTL`.\n\n---\n\n## 5. Alternatives Considered\n\n| Option                                      | Reason Rejected                                                 |\n|---------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------|\n| **“Always-on” ECS / Kubernetes fleet**      | Fails idle-cost constraint; heavier ops overhead.               |\n| **Agones-based dedicated game servers**     | Optimised for fast-twitch games; not multi-tenant friendly.     |\n| **Pure Lambda (no orchestrator)**           | Hard to manage complex error-handling, versioning, fan-out.     |\n| **EventBridge Pipes without Step Functions**| Lacks step-level retries, wait-state semantics, & observability.|\n\n---\n\n## 6. Links\n\n* [AWS Docs – Step Functions Best Practices](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/step-functions/latest/dg/bp.html)  \n* [ServerlessLand Pattern – WebSocket to Step Functions](https://serverlessland.com/patterns/apigw-websocket-stepfunctions)  \n* [Gamasutra Post – “Deterministic Lock-Step in Cloud”](https://example.com/deterministic-lockstep)  \n\n---\n\n## 7. Decisions Revisited\n\nWe will revisit this ADR when either:\n\n1. AWS releases **Lambda SnapStart for Python** (cold-start relief), or  \n2.  > 20 % of game loops exceed the 900 ms limit, indicating fragmentation overhead.\n\n---\n```", "numLines": 200, "startLine": 1, "totalLines": 200}}

1→```python
     2→"""
     3→ledgerquest_engine/ecs/dynamodb_repository.py
     4→
     5→A production-grade DynamoDB–backed implementation of the Entity–Component–System
     6→persistence layer used by LedgerQuest Engine.
     7→
     8→This repository persists entities and their associated components into a
     9→single DynamoDB table using a composite primary key:
    10→
    11→    PK  = "ENTITY#{tenant_id}#{entity_id}"
    12→    SK  = "COMPONENT#{component_name}"
    13→
    14→The design satisfies the following requirements:
    15→
    16→    • Multi-tenant isolation                (tenant_id included in the PK)
    17→    • Strongly-typed component marshaling   (dataclasses + Pydantic models)
    18→    • Auditable change history              (ConditionExpression + EventBridge)
    19→    • Optimistic concurrency control        (entity_version attribute)
    20→    • Incremental component loading         (query on PK, filter on SK)
    21→
    22→The module is intentionally self-contained so that it can be imported by both
    23→Lambda functions (per-request ECS mutations) and long-running game-loop
    24→simulations scheduled in Fargate tasks.
    25→
    26→Functions are synchronous for readability, but boto3 client calls are executed
    27→using a ThreadPool so the interface remains non-blocking when used from
    28→async/await code.
    29→"""
    30→
    31→from __future__ import annotations
    32→
    33→import json
    34→import logging
    35→import os
    36→from concurrent.futures import ThreadPoolExecutor
    37→from dataclasses import asdict, dataclass, field, is_dataclass
    38→from datetime import datetime, timezone
    39→from functools import partial
    40→from typing import Any, Dict, Iterable, List, Mapping, Optional, Type, TypeVar
    41→
    42→import boto3
    43→from botocore.exceptions import ClientError
    44→from pydantic import BaseModel, ValidationError
    45→
    46→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
    47→# Logging
    48→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
    49→
    50→_LOGGER = logging.getLogger("ledgerquest.ecs.dynamodb_repository")
    51→_LOGGER.setLevel(logging.INFO)
    52→
    53→
    54→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
    55→# Exceptions
    56→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
    57→
    58→
    59→class ECSRepositoryError(RuntimeError):
    60→    """Base class for repository related failures."""
    61→
    62→
    63→class EntityNotFound(ECSRepositoryError):
    64→    """Raised when an entity cannot be found in the database."""
    65→
    66→
    67→class ComponentValidationError(ECSRepositoryError):
    68→    """Raised when a component payload fails validation."""
    69→
    70→
    71→class OptimisticLockError(ECSRepositoryError):
    72→    """Raised when the entity_version no longer matches."""
    73→
    74→
    75→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
    76→# Utility / Typing
    77→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
    78→
    79→_T = TypeVar("_T", bound="ComponentModel")
    80→
    81→
    82→class ComponentModel(BaseModel):
    83→    """Base class for any ECS Component stored in DynamoDB."""
    84→
    85→    # Every component inherits tenant_id / entity_id so it can be reconstructed
    86→    tenant_id: str
    87→    entity_id: str
    88→
    89→    def pk(self) -> str:
    90→        return f"ENTITY#{self.tenant_id}#{self.entity_id}"
    91→
    92→    @property
    93→    def sk(self) -> str:
    94→        return f"COMPONENT#{self.__class__.__name__}"
    95→
    96→    class Config:
    97→        extra = "forbid"
    98→
    99→
   100→@dataclass(frozen=True)
   101→class EntityKey:
   102→    tenant_id: str
   103→    entity_id: str
   104→
   105→    def pk(self) -> str:
   106→        return f"ENTITY#{self.tenant_id}#{self.entity_id}"
   107→
   108→
   109→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   110→# Repository
   111→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   112→
   113→
   114→class DynamoDBECSRepository:
   115→    """
   116→    Reads / writes ECS entities into a DynamoDB table.
   117→
   118→    A single instance is safe for concurrent use between threads *provided* that
   119→    the AWS SDK client is not mutated at runtime.
   120→    """
   121→
   122→    _DEFAULT_CONCURRENCY = int(os.getenv("LEDGERQUEST_ECS_MAX_THREADS", "8"))
   123→
   124→    def __init__(
   125→        self,
   126→        table_name: str,
   127→        boto3_resource: Optional[Any] = None,
   128→        eventbridge_bus: Optional[str] = None,
   129→        executor: Optional[ThreadPoolExecutor] = None,
   130→    ) -> None:
   131→        self._dynamodb = boto3_resource or boto3.resource("dynamodb")
   132→        self._table = self._dynamodb.Table(table_name)
   133→        self._bus_name = eventbridge_bus
   134→        self._event_client = boto3.client("events") if eventbridge_bus else None
   135→        self._executor = executor or ThreadPoolExecutor(
   136→            max_workers=self._DEFAULT_CONCURRENCY
   137→        )
   138→        _LOGGER.debug(
   139→            "DynamoDBECSRepository initialised for table=%s, bus=%s",
   140→            table_name,
   141→            eventbridge_bus,
   142→        )
   143→
   144→    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   145→    # Public API
   146→    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   147→
   148→    def create_entity(
   149→        self, key: EntityKey, components: Iterable[ComponentModel]
   150→    ) -> None:
   151→        """
   152→        Insert a new entity; fails if the entity_id already exists for the
   153→        tenant. All components are stored in a single TransactWrite.
   154→
   155→        Raises:
   156→            ECSRepositoryError – on any DynamoDB failure
   157→            ComponentValidationError – if component fails validation
   158→        """
   159→        timestamp = datetime.now(tz=timezone.utc).isoformat()
   160→        transact_items: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []
   161→
   162→        for component in components:
   163→            component_dict = _serialise_component(component, timestamp)
   164→            transact_items.append(
   165→                {
   166→                    "Put": {
   167→                        "TableName": self._table.name,
   168→                        "Item": component_dict,
   169→                        "ConditionExpression": "attribute_not_exists(PK)",
   170→                    }
   171→                }
   172→            )
   173→
   174→        # entity_version item
   175→        entity_meta = {
   176→            "PK": key.pk(),
   177→            "SK": "META",
   178→            "entity_version": 1,
   179→            "created_at": timestamp,
   180→            "updated_at": timestamp,
   181→        }
   182→        transact_items.append(
   183→            {
   184→                "Put": {
   185→                    "TableName": self._table.name,
   186→                    "Item": entity_meta,
   187→                    "ConditionExpression": "attribute_not_exists(PK)",
   188→                }
   189→            }
   190→        )
   191→
   192→        _LOGGER.debug("Creating entity %s with %d components", key, len(components))
   193→        self._execute_transact_write(transact_items)
   194→        self._publish_event(
   195→            "EntityCreated", {"tenantId": key.tenant_id, "entityId": key.entity_id}
   196→        )
   197→
   198→    def get_components(
   199→        self,
   200→        key: EntityKey,
   201→        component_types: Optional[Iterable[Type[_T]]] = None,
   202→    ) -> Dict[str, _T]:
   203→        """
   204→        Load components for an entity. If `component_types` is provided the query
   205→        will filter accordingly (client-side).
   206→
   207→        Raises:
   208→            EntityNotFound
   209→        """
   210→        response = self._table.query(KeyConditionExpression="PK = :pk", ExpressionAttributeValues={":pk": key.pk()})
   211→        if not response.get("Items"):
   212→            raise EntityNotFound(f"Entity {key} not found")
   213→
   214→        components: Dict[str, _T] = {}
   215→        allowed_names = {c.__name__ for c in component_types} if component_types else None
   216→
   217→        for item in response["Items"]:
   218→            if item["SK"] == "META":
   219→                continue  # skip meta row
   220→            comp_name = item["SK"].split("#", 1)[1]
   221→            if allowed_names is None or comp_name in allowed_names:
   222→                comp_cls = _component_registry().get(comp_name)
   223→                if not comp_cls:
   224→                    _LOGGER.warning("Component class %s not registered", comp_name)
   225→                    continue
   226→                components[comp_name] = comp_cls.parse_obj(item["data"])  # type: ignore
   227→
   228→        return components
   229→
   230→    def put_component(
   231→        self,
   232→        component: ComponentModel,
   233→        expected_entity_version: Optional[int] = None,
   234→    ) -> None:
   235→        """
   236→        Inserts or replaces a single component.
   237→
   238→        Args:
   239→            component: ComponentModel – the component data.
   240→            expected_entity_version: int | None – optional optimistic lock.
   241→
   242→        Raises:
   243→            OptimisticLockError – entity version mismatch.
   244→        """
   245→        timestamp = datetime.now(tz=timezone.utc).isoformat()
   246→        transact_items: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []
   247→
   248→        # Upsert component
   249→        component_dict = _serialise_component(component, timestamp)
   250→        transact_items.append(
   251→            {
   252→                "Put": {
   253→                    "TableName": self._table.name,
   254→                    "Item": component_dict,
   255→                }
   256→            }
   257→        )
   258→
   259→        # Bump entity version
   260→        update_expression = "SET entity_version = entity_version + :inc, updated_at = :ts"
   261→        expression_values = {":inc": 1, ":ts": timestamp}
   262→        condition_expression = None
   263→        if expected_entity_version is not None:
   264→            condition_expression = "entity_version = :expected"
   265→            expression_values[":expected"] = expected_entity_version
   266→
   267→        transact_items.append(
   268→            {
   269→                "Update": {
   270→                    "TableName": self._table.name,
   271→                    "Key": {"PK": component.pk(), "SK": "META"},
   272→                    "UpdateExpression": update_expression,
   273→                    "ExpressionAttributeValues": expression_values,
   274→                    **(
   275→                        {"ConditionExpression": condition_expression}
   276→                        if condition_expression
   277→                        else {}
   278→                    ),
   279→                }
   280→            }
   281→        )
   282→
   283→        try:
   284→            self._execute_transact_write(transact_items)
   285→        except ClientError as exc:
   286→            if _is_conditional_failed(exc):
   287→                raise OptimisticLockError(
   288→                    f"Entity version mismatch for {component.entity_id}"
   289→                ) from exc
   290→            raise
   291→
   292→        self._publish_event(
   293→            "ComponentUpserted",
   294→            {
   295→                "tenantId": component.tenant_id,
   296→                "entityId": component.entity_id,
   297→                "component": component.__class__.__name__,
   298→            },
   299→        )
   300→
   301→    def delete_entity(self, key: EntityKey) -> None:
   302→        """
   303→        Delete an entity and all its components using a PartiQL statement.
   304→
   305→        Raises:
   306→            EntityNotFound – if the entity does not exist.
   307→        """
   308→        # Verify entity exists
   309→        try:
   310→            self._table.get_item(Key={"PK": key.pk(), "SK": "META"})["Item"]
   311→        except KeyError:
   312→            raise EntityNotFound(f"Entity {key} not found")
   313→
   314→        _LOGGER.debug("Deleting entity %s", key)
   315→        statement = f"DELETE FROM \"{self._table.name}\" WHERE PK=?"
   316→        self._table.meta.client.execute_statement(Statement=statement, Parameters=[{"S": key.pk()}])
   317→        self._publish_event(
   318→            "EntityDeleted", {"tenantId": key.tenant_id, "entityId": key.entity_id}
   319→        )
   320→
   321→    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   322→    # Internal helpers
   323→    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   324→
   325→    def _execute_transact_write(self, items: List[Dict[str, Any]]) -> None:
   326→        try:
   327→            future = self._executor.submit(
   328→                self._table.meta.client.transact_write_items, TransactItems=items
   329→            )
   330→            future.result()
   331→        except ClientError as exc:
   332→            _LOGGER.exception("DynamoDB TransactWrite failed: %s", exc)
   333→            raise ECSRepositoryError("DynamoDB transact write failed") from exc
   334→
   335→    def _publish_event(self, event_type: str, detail: Mapping[str, Any]) -> None:
   336→        if not self._event_client:
   337→            return  # EventBridge not configured
   338→        try:
   339→            self._event_client.put_events(
   340→                Entries=[
   341→                    {
   342→                        "Source": "ledgerquest.ecs",
   343→                        "DetailType": event_type,
   344→                        "Detail": json.dumps(detail),
   345→                        "EventBusName": self._bus_name,
   346→                        "Time": datetime.now(tz=timezone.utc),
   347→                    }
   348→                ]
   349→            )
   350→        except ClientError as exc:
   351→            # Fail silently – publishing is best-effort
   352→            _LOGGER.warning("EventBridge publish failed: %s", exc)
   353→
   354→
   355→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   356→# Component (De)Serialisation
   357→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   358→
   359→
   360→def _serialise_component(component: ComponentModel, timestamp: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:
   361→    if not isinstance(component, ComponentModel):
   362→        raise TypeError("component must inherit from ComponentModel")
   363→    try:
   364→        payload = component.dict(by_alias=True)
   365→    except ValidationError as exc:
   366→        raise ComponentValidationError(str(exc)) from exc
   367→    return {
   368→        "PK": component.pk(),
   369→        "SK": component.sk,
   370→        "data": payload,
   371→        "updated_at": timestamp,
   372→    }
   373→
   374→
   375→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   376→# Component Registry
   377→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   378→
   379→_registry: Dict[str, Type[ComponentModel]] = {}
   380→
   381→
   382→def register_component(cls: Type[_T]) -> Type[_T]:
   383→    """
   384→    Class decorator registering a ComponentModel subclass for automatic
   385→    deserialisation. The decorated class *must* inherit from ComponentModel.
   386→    """
   387→    if not issubclass(cls, ComponentModel):
   388→        raise TypeError("Only ComponentModel subclasses can be registered")
   389→    _registry[cls.__name__] = cls
   390→    return cls
   391→
   392→
   393→def _component_registry() -> Mapping[str, Type[ComponentModel]]:
   394→    return _registry
   395→
   396→
   397→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   398→# Helpers
   399→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   400→
   401→
   402→def _is_conditional_failed(exc: ClientError) -> bool:
   403→    return (
   404→        exc.response.get("Error", {}).get("Code") == "TransactionCanceledException"
   405→        and any(
   406→            reason.get("Code") == "ConditionalCheckFailed"
   407→            for reason in exc.response.get("CancellationReasons", [])
   408→        )
   409→    )
   410→
   411→
   412→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   413→# Example Component Definitions
   414→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   415→
   416→# The following component classes *serve as usage examples* and *may* be moved
   417→# into their own modules by consumers of this repository.
   418→
   419→
   420→@register_component
   421→class Position(ComponentModel):
   422→    x: float
   423→    y: float
   424→    z: float = 0.0
   425→
   426→
   427→@register_component
   428→class Health(ComponentModel):
   429→    max_hp: int = 100
   430→    current_hp: int = 100
   431→    last_damage_source: Optional[str] = None
   432→
   433→
   434→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   435→# Convenience – quick smoke test
   436→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   437→
   438→if __name__ == "__main__" and os.getenv("LEDGERQUEST_ECS_SMOKE_TEST"):
   439→    import uuid
   440→    from pprint import pprint
   441→
   442→    logging.basicConfig(level=logging.DEBUG)
   443→
   444→    repo = DynamoDBECSRepository(
   445→        table_name=os.getenv("LEDGERQUEST_ECS_TABLE", "LedgerQuestECS"),
   446→        eventbridge_bus=os.getenv("LEDGERQUEST_ECS_BUS", None),
   447→    )
   448→
   449→    tenant = "demo"
   450→    eid = str(uuid.uuid4())[:8]
   451→    key = EntityKey(tenant_id=tenant, entity_id=eid)
   452→
   453→    _LOGGER.info("Creating entity %s", key)
   454→    repo.create_entity(
   455→        key,
   456→        components=[
   457→            Position(tenant_id=tenant, entity_id=eid, x=1.0, y=1.0),
   458→            Health(tenant_id=tenant, entity_id=eid),
   459→        ],
   460→    )
   461→
   462→    _LOGGER.info("Fetching components")
   463→    comps = repo.get_components(key)
   464→    pprint(comps)
   465→
   466→    _LOGGER.info("Updating position")
   467→    repo.put_component(
   468→        Position(tenant_id=tenant, entity_id=eid, x=5.0, y=7.5, z=1.2),
   469→        expected_entity_version=1,
   470→    )
   471→
   472→    _LOGGER.info("Deleting entity")
   473→    repo.delete_entity(key)
   474→```

   self, key: EntityKey, components: Iterable[ComponentModel]\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Insert a new entity; fails if the entity_id already exists for the\n        tenant. All components are stored in a single TransactWrite.\n\n        Raises:\n            ECSRepositoryError – on any DynamoDB failure\n            ComponentValidationError – if component fails validation\n        \"\"\"\n        timestamp = datetime.now(tz=timezone.utc).isoformat()\n        transact_items: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []\n\n        for component in components:\n            component_dict = _serialise_component(component, timestamp)\n            transact_items.append(\n                {\n                    \"Put\": {\n                        \"TableName\": self._table.name,\n                        \"Item\": component_dict,\n                        \"ConditionExpression\": \"attribute_not_exists(PK)\",\n                    }\n                }\n            )\n\n        # entity_version item\n        entity_meta = {\n            \"PK\": key.pk(),\n            \"SK\": \"META\",\n            \"entity_version\": 1,\n            \"created_at\": timestamp,\n            \"updated_at\": timestamp,\n        }\n        transact_items.append(\n            {\n                \"Put\": {\n                    \"TableName\": self._table.name,\n                    \"Item\": entity_meta,\n                    \"ConditionExpression\": \"attribute_not_exists(PK)\",\n                }\n            }\n        )\n\n        _LOGGER.debug(\"Creating entity %s with %d components\", key, len(components))\n        self._execute_transact_write(transact_items)\n        self._publish_event(\n            \"EntityCreated\", {\"tenantId\": key.tenant_id, \"entityId\": key.entity_id}\n        )\n\n    def get_components(\n        self,\n        key: EntityKey,\n        component_types: Optional[Iterable[Type[_T]]] = None,\n    ) -> Dict[str, _T]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Load components for an entity. If `component_types` is provided the query\n        will filter accordingly (client-side).\n\n        Raises:\n            EntityNotFound\n        \"\"\"\n        response = self._table.query(KeyConditionExpression=\"PK = :pk\", ExpressionAttributeValues={\":pk\": key.pk()})\n        if not response.get(\"Items\"):\n            raise EntityNotFound(f\"Entity {key} not found\")\n\n        components: Dict[str, _T] = {}\n        allowed_names = {c.__name__ for c in component_types} if component_types else None\n\n        for item in response[\"Items\"]:\n            if item[\"SK\"] == \"META\":\n                continue  # skip meta row\n            comp_name = item[\"SK\"].split(\"#\", 1)[1]\n            if allowed_names is None or comp_name in allowed_names:\n                comp_cls = _component_registry().get(comp_name)\n                if not comp_cls:\n                    _LOGGER.warning(\"Component class %s not registered\", comp_name)\n                    continue\n                components[comp_name] = comp_cls.parse_obj(item[\"data\"])  # type: ignore\n\n        return components\n\n    def put_component(\n        self,\n        component: ComponentModel,\n        expected_entity_version: Optional[int] = None,\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Inserts or replaces a single component.\n\n        Args:\n            component: ComponentModel – the component data.\n            expected_entity_version: int | None – optional optimistic lock.\n\n        Raises:\n            OptimisticLockError – entity version mismatch.\n        \"\"\"\n        timestamp = datetime.now(tz=timezone.utc).isoformat()\n        transact_items: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []\n\n        # Upsert component\n        component_dict = _serialise_component(component, timestamp)\n        transact_items.append(\n            {\n                \"Put\": {\n                    \"TableName\": self._table.name,\n                    \"Item\": component_dict,\n                }\n            }\n        )\n\n        # Bump entity version\n        update_expression = \"SET entity_version = entity_version + :inc, updated_at = :ts\"\n        expression_values = {\":inc\": 1, \":ts\": timestamp}\n        condition_expression = None\n        if expected_entity_version is not None:\n            condition_expression = \"entity_version = :expected\"\n            expression_values[\":expected\"] = expected_entity_version\n\n        transact_items.append(\n            {\n                \"Update\": {\n                    \"TableName\": self._table.name,\n                    \"Key\": {\"PK\": component.pk(), \"SK\": \"META\"},\n                    \"UpdateExpression\": update_expression,\n                    \"ExpressionAttributeValues\": expression_values,\n                    **(\n                        {\"ConditionExpression\": condition_expression}\n                        if condition_expression\n                        else {}\n                    ),\n                }\n            }\n        )\n\n        try:\n            self._execute_transact_write(transact_items)\n        except ClientError as exc:\n            if _is_conditional_failed(exc):\n                raise OptimisticLockError(\n                    f\"Entity version mismatch for {component.entity_id}\"\n                ) from exc\n            raise\n\n        self._publish_event(\n            \"ComponentUpserted\",\n            {\n                \"tenantId\": component.tenant_id,\n                \"entityId\": component.entity_id,\n                \"component\": component.__class__.__name__,\n            },\n        )\n\n    def delete_entity(self, key: EntityKey) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Delete an entity and all its components using a PartiQL statement.\n\n        Raises:\n            EntityNotFound – if the entity does not exist.\n        \"\"\"\n        # Verify entity exists\n        try:\n            self._table.get_item(Key={\"PK\": key.pk(), \"SK\": \"META\"})[\"Item\"]\n        except KeyError:\n            raise EntityNotFound(f\"Entity {key} not found\")\n\n        _LOGGER.debug(\"Deleting entity %s\", key)\n        statement = f\"DELETE FROM \\\"{self._table.name}\\\" WHERE PK=?\"\n        self._table.meta.client.execute_statement(Statement=statement, Parameters=[{\"S\": key.pk()}])\n        self._publish_event(\n            \"EntityDeleted\", {\"tenantId\": key.tenant_id, \"entityId\": key.entity_id}\n        )\n\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    # Internal helpers\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n\n    def _execute_transact_write(self, items: List[Dict[str, Any]]) -> None:\n        try:\n            future = self._executor.submit(\n                self._table.meta.client.transact_write_items, TransactItems=items\n            )\n            future.result()\n        except ClientError as exc:\n            _LOGGER.exception(\"DynamoDB TransactWrite failed: %s\", exc)\n            raise ECSRepositoryError(\"DynamoDB transact write failed\") from exc\n\n    def _publish_event(self, event_type: str, detail: Mapping[str, Any]) -> None:\n        if not self._event_client:\n            return  # EventBridge not configured\n        try:\n            self._event_client.put_events(\n                Entries=[\n                    {\n                        \"Source\": \"ledgerquest.ecs\",\n                        \"DetailType\": event_type,\n                        \"Detail\": json.dumps(detail),\n                        \"EventBusName\": self._bus_name,\n                        \"Time\": datetime.now(tz=timezone.utc),\n                    }\n                ]\n            )\n        except ClientError as exc:\n            # Fail silently – publishing is best-effort\n            _LOGGER.warning(\"EventBridge publish failed: %s\", exc)\n\n\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n# Component (De)Serialisation\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n\n\ndef _serialise_component(component: ComponentModel, timestamp: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n    if not isinstance(component, ComponentModel):\n        raise TypeError(\"component must inherit from ComponentModel\")\n    try:\n        payload = component.dict(by_alias=True)\n    except ValidationError as exc:\n        raise ComponentValidationError(str(exc)) from exc\n    return {\n        \"PK\": component.pk(),\n        \"SK\": component.sk,\n        \"data\": payload,\n        \"updated_at\": timestamp,\n    }\n\n\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n# Component Registry\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n\n_registry: Dict[str, Type[ComponentModel]] = {}\n\n\ndef register_component(cls: Type[_T]) -> Type[_T]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Class decorator registering a ComponentModel subclass for automatic\n    deserialisation. The decorated class *must* inherit from ComponentModel.\n    \"\"\"\n    if not issubclass(cls, ComponentModel):\n        raise TypeError(\"Only ComponentModel subclasses can be registered\")\n    _registry[cls.__name__] = cls\n    return cls\n\n\ndef _component_registry() -> Mapping[str, Type[ComponentModel]]:\n    return _registry\n\n\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n# Helpers\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n\n\ndef _is_conditional_failed(exc: ClientError) -> bool:\n    return (\n        exc.response.get(\"Error\", {}).get(\"Code\") == \"TransactionCanceledException\"\n        and any(\n            reason.get(\"Code\") == \"ConditionalCheckFailed\"\n            for reason in exc.response.get(\"CancellationReasons\", [])\n        )\n    )\n\n\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n# Example Component Definitions\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n\n# The following component classes *serve as usage examples* and *may* be moved\n# into their own modules by consumers of this repository.\n\n\n@register_component\nclass Position(ComponentModel):\n    x: float\n    y: float\n    z: float = 0.0\n\n\n@register_component\nclass Health(ComponentModel):\n    max_hp: int = 100\n    current_hp: int = 100\n    last_damage_source: Optional[str] = None\n\n\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n# Convenience – quick smoke test\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\" and os.getenv(\"LEDGERQUEST_ECS_SMOKE_TEST\"):\n    import uuid\n    from pprint import pprint\n\n    logging.basicConfig(level=logging.DEBUG)\n\n    repo = DynamoDBECSRepository(\n        table_name=os.getenv(\"LEDGERQUEST_ECS_TABLE\", \"LedgerQuestECS\"),\n        eventbridge_bus=os.getenv(\"LEDGERQUEST_ECS_BUS\", None),\n    )\n\n    tenant = \"demo\"\n    eid = str(uuid.uuid4())[:8]\n    key = EntityKey(tenant_id=tenant, entity_id=eid)\n\n    _LOGGER.info(\"Creating entity %s\", key)\n    repo.create_entity(\n        key,\n        components=[\n            Position(tenant_id=tenant, entity_id=eid, x=1.0, y=1.0),\n            Health(tenant_id=tenant, entity_id=eid),\n        ],\n    )\n\n    _LOGGER.info(\"Fetching components\")\n    comps = repo.get_components(key)\n    pprint(comps)\n\n    _LOGGER.info(\"Updating position\")\n    repo.put_component(\n        Position(tenant_id=tenant, entity_id=eid, x=5.0, y=7.5, z=1.2),\n        expected_entity_version=1,\n    )\n\n    _LOGGER.info(\"Deleting entity\")\n    repo.delete_entity(key)\n```", "numLines": 474, "startLine": 1, "totalLines": 474}}

1→```python
     2→"""
     3→LedgerQuest Engine – Game-Loop State-Machine Definition
     4→------------------------------------------------------
     5→
     6→This module builds and (optionally) deploys the core game-loop AWS Step Functions
     7→state machine used by the LedgerQuest Engine.  The state machine orchestrates
     8→each frame-tick by invoking small, stateless Lambda functions that operate on
     9→externalised entity data (DynamoDB, S3, etc.).  The design embraces the classic
    10→game-loop phases—input, AI, physics, rendering, and persistence—while staying
    11→completely serverless and horizontally scalable.
    12→
    13→Why is this file a *.asl.json in source control but a Python module here?
    14→---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    15→The CI pipeline expects a pure ASL JSON file in `infra/step_functions`, but
    16→authoring complex ASL by hand is error-prone.  This Python module generates the
    17→same JSON deterministically, enables compile-time linting, strong typing, and
    18→unit tests, and can even bootstrap/deploy the state machine via `boto3`.
    19→
    20→Running the module directly will:
    21→    1. Emit the ASL JSON to the canonical path expected by CDK/Terraform.
    22→    2. Optionally create/update the Step Function in the target AWS account
    23→       when the `--deploy` flag is provided.
    24→"""
    25→
    26→from __future__ import annotations
    27→
    28→import argparse
    29→import json
    30→import logging
    31→import os
    32→import sys
    33→from dataclasses import asdict, dataclass
    34→from datetime import datetime, timezone
    35→from pathlib import Path
    36→from typing import Any, Dict
    37→
    38→import boto3
    39→from botocore.exceptions import BotoCoreError, ClientError
    40→
    41→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
    42→# Configuration dataclass                                                     #
    43→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
    44→
    45→
    46→@dataclass(frozen=True)
    47→class LambdaArns:
    48→    """Fully-qualified ARNs for the Lambda functions invoked by the state machine."""
    49→
    50→    process_input: str
    51→    update_ai: str
    52→    physics_step: str
    53→    render_frame: str
    54→    persist_state: str
    55→
    56→
    57→@dataclass(frozen=True)
    58→class GameLoopStateMachineConfig:
    59→    """
    60→    All inputs required to generate and/or deploy the state machine definition.
    61→    """
    62→
    63→    lambda_arns: LambdaArns
    64→    role_arn: str  # IAM role assumed by Step Functions
    65→    state_machine_name: str = "LedgerQuest-GameLoop"
    66→    tick_rate_hz: int = 30  # Target number of game-loop iterations per second
    67→
    68→    # Output path for the generated ASL file (overridden in CI for artefact store)
    69→    output_path: Path = Path(__file__).with_suffix(".asl.json")
    70→
    71→
    72→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
    73→# State machine builder                                                       #
    74→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
    75→
    76→
    77→def _seconds_per_tick(tick_rate_hz: int) -> float:
    78→    """Small helper to convert a tick rate to a Step Functions-compatible duration."""
    79→    return max(1.0 / tick_rate_hz, 0.001)  # SFN minimum is 0.001 seconds
    80→
    81→
    82→def build_state_machine(cfg: GameLoopStateMachineConfig) -> Dict[str, Any]:
    83→    """
    84→    Return the Amazon States Language (ASL) document for the LedgerQuest game loop.
    85→
    86→    The state machine is intentionally linear and deterministic to guarantee
    87→    strict ordering of mutations against entity state in DynamoDB.
    88→    """
    89→    # Shorthand for readability
    90→    lbd = cfg.lambda_arns
    91→
    92→    seconds_per_tick = _seconds_per_tick(cfg.tick_rate_hz)
    93→
    94→    definition = {
    95→        "Comment": "LedgerQuest Engine – Single-Shard Game Loop.",
    96→        "StartAt": "InitializeFrame",
    97→        "Version": "1.0",
    98→        "TimeoutSeconds": 60,
    99→        "States": {
   100→            "InitializeFrame": {
   101→                "Type": "Pass",
   102→                "ResultPath": "$.metadata",
   103→                "Result": {
   104→                    "frameStartEpochMs.$": "$$.State.EnteredTime",
   105→                    "tickRateHz": cfg.tick_rate_hz,
   106→                },
   107→                "Next": "ProcessInput",
   108→            },
   109→            "ProcessInput": _lambda_task_state(
   110→                name="ProcessInput",
   111→                function_arn=lbd.process_input,
   112→                next_state="UpdateAI",
   113→            ),
   114→            "UpdateAI": _lambda_task_state(
   115→                name="UpdateAI",
   116→                function_arn=lbd.update_ai,
   117→                next_state="PhysicsStep",
   118→            ),
   119→            "PhysicsStep": _lambda_task_state(
   120→                name="PhysicsStep",
   121→                function_arn=lbd.physics_step,
   122→                next_state="RenderFrame",
   123→            ),
   124→            "RenderFrame": _lambda_task_state(
   125→                name="RenderFrame",
   126→                function_arn=lbd.render_frame,
   127→                next_state="PersistState",
   128→            ),
   129→            "PersistState": _lambda_task_state(
   130→                name="PersistState",
   131→                function_arn=lbd.persist_state,
   132→                next_state="WaitForNextTick",
   133→            ),
   134→            "WaitForNextTick": {
   135→                "Type": "Wait",
   136→                "Seconds": seconds_per_tick,
   137→                "Next": "LoopDecider",
   138→            },
   139→            "LoopDecider": {
   140→                "Type": "Choice",
   141→                "Choices": [
   142→                    {
   143→                        "Variable": "$.continue",
   144→                        "BooleanEquals": True,
   145→                        "Next": "InitializeFrame",
   146→                    }
   147→                ],
   148→                "Default": "GameFinished",
   149→            },
   150→            "GameFinished": {
   151→                "Type": "Succeed",
   152→                "Comment": "Game ended normally.",
   153→            },
   154→        },
   155→    }
   156→    return definition
   157→
   158→
   159→def _lambda_task_state(
   160→    *, name: str, function_arn: str, next_state: str
   161→) -> Dict[str, Any]:
   162→    """
   163→    DRY helper to create a Lambda task state with common error handling and
   164→    exponential back-off strategy.
   165→    """
   166→    return {
   167→        "Type": "Task",
   168→        "Resource": "arn:aws:states:::lambda:invoke",
   169→        "Parameters": {
   170→            "FunctionName": function_arn,
   171→            "Payload.$": "$",
   172→        },
   173→        "TimeoutSeconds": 10,
   174→        "Retry": [
   175→            {
   176→                "ErrorEquals": ["Lambda.ServiceException", "Lambda.AWSLambdaException"],
   177→                "IntervalSeconds": 2,
   178→                "MaxAttempts": 6,
   179→                "BackoffRate": 2.0,
   180→            }
   181→        ],
   182→        "Catch": [
   183→            {
   184→                "ErrorEquals": ["States.ALL"],
   185→                "ResultPath": "$.error",
   186→                "Next": "GameFinished",
   187→            }
   188→        ],
   189→        "Next": next_state,
   190→    }
   191→
   192→
   193→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   194→# JSON emission & deployment helpers                                          #
   195→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   196→
   197→
   198→def write_asl(cfg: GameLoopStateMachineConfig, definition: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:
   199→    """Persist the generated ASL to disk (pretty-printed and sorted)."""
   200→    cfg.output_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   201→    with cfg.output_path.open("w", encoding="utf-8") as fp:
   202→        json.dump(definition, fp, indent=2, sort_keys=True)
   203→    logging.info("Wrote state-machine ASL to %s", cfg.output_path)
   204→
   205→
   206→def deploy_state_machine(cfg: GameLoopStateMachineConfig, definition: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:
   207→    """
   208→    Create or update the Step Function in AWS, idempotently.
   209→
   210→    Requires AWS credentials configured via environment variables, instance
   211→    profile, or ~/.aws/credentials.
   212→    """
   213→    client = boto3.client("stepfunctions")
   214→
   215→    # Attempt to find an existing state machine by name.
   216→    try:
   217→        paginator = client.get_paginator("list_state_machines")
   218→        for page in paginator.paginate():
   219→            match = next(
   220→                (sm for sm in page["stateMachines"] if sm["name"] == cfg.state_machine_name),
   221→                None,
   222→            )
   223→            if match:
   224→                _update_state_machine(client, match["stateMachineArn"], cfg, definition)
   225→                return
   226→    except (BotoCoreError, ClientError) as ex:
   227→        logging.error("Unable to list Step Functions: %s", ex)
   228→        raise SystemExit(2)
   229→
   230→    # Not found → create new
   231→    try:
   232→        resp = client.create_state_machine(
   233→            name=cfg.state_machine_name,
   234→            definition=json.dumps(definition),
   235→            roleArn=cfg.role_arn,
   236→            type="STANDARD",  # or "EXPRESS" if latency is critical
   237→            tags=[{"key": "Project", "value": "LedgerQuest"}],
   238→        )
   239→        logging.info("Created new state machine %s", resp["stateMachineArn"])
   240→    except (BotoCoreError, ClientError) as ex:
   241→        logging.error("Unable to create Step Function: %s", ex)
   242→        raise SystemExit(2)
   243→
   244→
   245→def _update_state_machine(
   246→    client: "boto3.client",
   247→    state_machine_arn: str,
   248→    cfg: GameLoopStateMachineConfig,
   249→    definition: Dict[str, Any],
   250→) -> None:
   251→    """Helper to update an existing state machine with a new definition."""
   252→    try:
   253→        client.update_state_machine(
   254→            stateMachineArn=state_machine_arn,
   255→            definition=json.dumps(definition),
   256→            roleArn=cfg.role_arn,
   257→        )
   258→        logging.info("Updated existing state machine %s", state_machine_arn)
   259→    except (BotoCoreError, ClientError) as ex:
   260→        logging.error("Unable to update Step Function: %s", ex)
   261→        raise SystemExit(2)
   262→
   263→
   264→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   265→# CLI                                                                         #
   266→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   267→
   268→def _parse_args() -> argparse.Namespace:
   269→    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Generate & deploy LedgerQuest game-loop state machine.")
   270→    parser.add_argument("--process-input-arn", required=True, help="Lambda ARN for the input-processing task")
   271→    parser.add_argument("--update-ai-arn", required=True, help="Lambda ARN for the AI update task")
   272→    parser.add_argument("--physics-step-arn", required=True, help="Lambda ARN for the physics step task")
   273→    parser.add_argument("--render-frame-arn", required=True, help="Lambda ARN for the frame rendering task")
   274→    parser.add_argument("--persist-state-arn", required=True, help="Lambda ARN for the state persistence task")
   275→    parser.add_argument("--iam-role-arn", required=True, help="IAM role ARN assumed by Step Functions")
   276→    parser.add_argument("--tick-rate", type=int, default=30, help="Target ticks per second (default 30)")
   277→    parser.add_argument("--state-machine-name", default="LedgerQuest-GameLoop")
   278→    parser.add_argument("--output", type=Path, help="Override output path for the ASL JSON")
   279→    parser.add_argument("--deploy", action="store_true", help="Create/Update the state machine in AWS")
   280→    parser.add_argument("-v", "--verbose", action="count", default=0)
   281→    return parser.parse_args()
   282→
   283→
   284→def main() -> None:
   285→    args = _parse_args()
   286→    logging.basicConfig(
   287→        level=logging.DEBUG if args.verbose else logging.INFO,
   288→        format="%(asctime)s  %(levelname)-8s %(message)s",
   289→        datefmt="%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S",
   290→    )
   291→
   292→    cfg = GameLoopStateMachineConfig(
   293→        lambda_arns=LambdaArns(
   294→            process_input=args.process_input_arn,
   295→            update_ai=args.update_ai_arn,
   296→            physics_step=args.physics_step_arn,
   297→            render_frame=args.render_frame_arn,
   298→            persist_state=args.persist_state_arn,
   299→        ),
   300→        role_arn=args.iam_role_arn,
   301→        state_machine_name=args.state_machine_name,
   302→        tick_rate_hz=args.tick_rate,
   303→        output_path=args.output or GameLoopStateMachineConfig.output_path,
   304→    )
   305→
   306→    definition = build_state_machine(cfg)
   307→    write_asl(cfg, definition)
   308→
   309→    if args.deploy:
   310→        deploy_state_machine(cfg, definition)
   311→
   312→
   313→if __name__ == "__main__":
   314→    # Sanity check that AWS credentials exist *before* building anything
   315→    if "AWS_REGION" not in os.environ and "AWS_DEFAULT_REGION" not in os.environ:
   316→        os.environ["AWS_DEFAULT_REGION"] = "us-east-1"
   317→        logging.warning("AWS region not specified; defaulting to us-east-1")
   318→
   319→    try:
   320→        main()
   321→    except KeyboardInterrupt:
   322→        logging.error("Interrupted by user")
   323→        sys.exit(130)
   324→```

botocore.exceptions import BotoCoreError, ClientError\n\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n# Configuration dataclass                                                     #\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n\n\n@dataclass(frozen=True)\nclass LambdaArns:\n    \"\"\"Fully-qualified ARNs for the Lambda functions invoked by the state machine.\"\"\"\n\n    process_input: str\n    update_ai: str\n    physics_step: str\n    render_frame: str\n    persist_state: str\n\n\n@dataclass(frozen=True)\nclass GameLoopStateMachineConfig:\n    \"\"\"\n    All inputs required to generate and/or deploy the state machine definition.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    lambda_arns: LambdaArns\n    role_arn: str  # IAM role assumed by Step Functions\n    state_machine_name: str = \"LedgerQuest-GameLoop\"\n    tick_rate_hz: int = 30  # Target number of game-loop iterations per second\n\n    # Output path for the generated ASL file (overridden in CI for artefact store)\n    output_path: Path = Path(__file__).with_suffix(\".asl.json\")\n\n\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n# State machine builder                                                       #\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n\n\ndef _seconds_per_tick(tick_rate_hz: int) -> float:\n    \"\"\"Small helper to convert a tick rate to a Step Functions-compatible duration.\"\"\"\n    return max(1.0 / tick_rate_hz, 0.001)  # SFN minimum is 0.001 seconds\n\n\ndef build_state_machine(cfg: GameLoopStateMachineConfig) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Return the Amazon States Language (ASL) document for the LedgerQuest game loop.\n\n    The state machine is intentionally linear and deterministic to guarantee\n    strict ordering of mutations against entity state in DynamoDB.\n    \"\"\"\n    # Shorthand for readability\n    lbd = cfg.lambda_arns\n\n    seconds_per_tick = _seconds_per_tick(cfg.tick_rate_hz)\n\n    definition = {\n        \"Comment\": \"LedgerQuest Engine – Single-Shard Game Loop.\",\n        \"StartAt\": \"InitializeFrame\",\n        \"Version\": \"1.0\",\n        \"TimeoutSeconds\": 60,\n        \"States\": {\n            \"InitializeFrame\": {\n                \"Type\": \"Pass\",\n                \"ResultPath\": \"$.metadata\",\n                \"Result\": {\n                    \"frameStartEpochMs.$\": \"$$.State.EnteredTime\",\n                    \"tickRateHz\": cfg.tick_rate_hz,\n                },\n                \"Next\": \"ProcessInput\",\n            },\n            \"ProcessInput\": _lambda_task_state(\n                name=\"ProcessInput\",\n                function_arn=lbd.process_input,\n                next_state=\"UpdateAI\",\n            ),\n            \"UpdateAI\": _lambda_task_state(\n                name=\"UpdateAI\",\n                function_arn=lbd.update_ai,\n                next_state=\"PhysicsStep\",\n            ),\n            \"PhysicsStep\": _lambda_task_state(\n                name=\"PhysicsStep\",\n                function_arn=lbd.physics_step,\n                next_state=\"RenderFrame\",\n            ),\n            \"RenderFrame\": _lambda_task_state(\n                name=\"RenderFrame\",\n                function_arn=lbd.render_frame,\n                next_state=\"PersistState\",\n            ),\n            \"PersistState\": _lambda_task_state(\n                name=\"PersistState\",\n                function_arn=lbd.persist_state,\n                next_state=\"WaitForNextTick\",\n            ),\n            \"WaitForNextTick\": {\n                \"Type\": \"Wait\",\n                \"Seconds\": seconds_per_tick,\n                \"Next\": \"LoopDecider\",\n            },\n            \"LoopDecider\": {\n                \"Type\": \"Choice\",\n                \"Choices\": [\n                    {\n                        \"Variable\": \"$.continue\",\n                        \"BooleanEquals\": True,\n                        \"Next\": \"InitializeFrame\",\n                    }\n                ],\n                \"Default\": \"GameFinished\",\n            },\n            \"GameFinished\": {\n                \"Type\": \"Succeed\",\n                \"Comment\": \"Game ended normally.\",\n            },\n        },\n    }\n    return definition\n\n\ndef _lambda_task_state(\n    *, name: str, function_arn: str, next_state: str\n) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n    \"\"\"\n    DRY helper to create a Lambda task state with common error handling and\n    exponential back-off strategy.\n    \"\"\"\n    return {\n        \"Type\": \"Task\",\n        \"Resource\": \"arn:aws:states:::lambda:invoke\",\n        \"Parameters\": {\n            \"FunctionName\": function_arn,\n            \"Payload.$\": \"$\",\n        },\n        \"TimeoutSeconds\": 10,\n        \"Retry\": [\n            {\n                \"ErrorEquals\": [\"Lambda.ServiceException\", \"Lambda.AWSLambdaException\"],\n                \"IntervalSeconds\": 2,\n                \"MaxAttempts\": 6,\n                \"BackoffRate\": 2.0,\n            }\n        ],\n        \"Catch\": [\n            {\n                \"ErrorEquals\": [\"States.ALL\"],\n                \"ResultPath\": \"$.error\",\n                \"Next\": \"GameFinished\",\n            }\n        ],\n        \"Next\": next_state,\n    }\n\n\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n# JSON emission & deployment helpers                                          #\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n\n\ndef write_asl(cfg: GameLoopStateMachineConfig, definition: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:\n    \"\"\"Persist the generated ASL to disk (pretty-printed and sorted).\"\"\"\n    cfg.output_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n    with cfg.output_path.open(\"w\", encoding=\"utf-8\") as fp:\n        json.dump(definition, fp, indent=2, sort_keys=True)\n    logging.info(\"Wrote state-machine ASL to %s\", cfg.output_path)\n\n\ndef deploy_state_machine(cfg: GameLoopStateMachineConfig, definition: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:\n    \"\"\"\n    Create or update the Step Function in AWS, idempotently.\n\n    Requires AWS credentials configured via environment variables, instance\n    profile, or ~/.aws/credentials.\n    \"\"\"\n    client = boto3.client(\"stepfunctions\")\n\n    # Attempt to find an existing state machine by name.\n    try:\n        paginator = client.get_paginator(\"list_state_machines\")\n        for page in paginator.paginate():\n            match = next(\n                (sm for sm in page[\"stateMachines\"] if sm[\"name\"] == cfg.state_machine_name),\n                None,\n            )\n            if match:\n                _update_state_machine(client, match[\"stateMachineArn\"], cfg, definition)\n                return\n    except (BotoCoreError, ClientError) as ex:\n        logging.error(\"Unable to list Step Functions: %s\", ex)\n        raise SystemExit(2)\n\n    # Not found → create new\n    try:\n        resp = client.create_state_machine(\n            name=cfg.state_machine_name,\n            definition=json.dumps(definition),\n            roleArn=cfg.role_arn,\n            type=\"STANDARD\",  # or \"EXPRESS\" if latency is critical\n            tags=[{\"key\": \"Project\", \"value\": \"LedgerQuest\"}],\n        )\n        logging.info(\"Created new state machine %s\", resp[\"stateMachineArn\"])\n    except (BotoCoreError, ClientError) as ex:\n        logging.error(\"Unable to create Step Function: %s\", ex)\n        raise SystemExit(2)\n\n\ndef _update_state_machine(\n    client: \"boto3.client\",\n    state_machine_arn: str,\n    cfg: GameLoopStateMachineConfig,\n    definition: Dict[str, Any],\n) -> None:\n    \"\"\"Helper to update an existing state machine with a new definition.\"\"\"\n    try:\n        client.update_state_machine(\n            stateMachineArn=state_machine_arn,\n            definition=json.dumps(definition),\n            roleArn=cfg.role_arn,\n        )\n        logging.info(\"Updated existing state machine %s\", state_machine_arn)\n    except (BotoCoreError, ClientError) as ex:\n        logging.error(\"Unable to update Step Function: %s\", ex)\n        raise SystemExit(2)\n\n\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n# CLI                                                                         #\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n\ndef _parse_args() -> argparse.Namespace:\n    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=\"Generate & deploy LedgerQuest game-loop state machine.\")\n    parser.add_argument(\"--process-input-arn\", required=True, help=\"Lambda ARN for the input-processing task\")\n    parser.add_argument(\"--update-ai-arn\", required=True, help=\"Lambda ARN for the AI update task\")\n    parser.add_argument(\"--physics-step-arn\", required=True, help=\"Lambda ARN for the physics step task\")\n    parser.add_argument(\"--render-frame-arn\", required=True, help=\"Lambda ARN for the frame rendering task\")\n    parser.add_argument(\"--persist-state-arn\", required=True, help=\"Lambda ARN for the state persistence task\")\n    parser.add_argument(\"--iam-role-arn\", required=True, help=\"IAM role ARN assumed by Step Functions\")\n    parser.add_argument(\"--tick-rate\", type=int, default=30, help=\"Target ticks per second (default 30)\")\n    parser.add_argument(\"--state-machine-name\", default=\"LedgerQuest-GameLoop\")\n    parser.add_argument(\"--output\", type=Path, help=\"Override output path for the ASL JSON\")\n    parser.add_argument(\"--deploy\", action=\"store_true\", help=\"Create/Update the state machine in AWS\")\n    parser.add_argument(\"-v\", \"--verbose\", action=\"count\", default=0)\n    return parser.parse_args()\n\n\ndef main() -> None:\n    args = _parse_args()\n    logging.basicConfig(\n        level=logging.DEBUG if args.verbose else logging.INFO,\n        format=\"%(asctime)s  %(levelname)-8s %(message)s\",\n        datefmt=\"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S\",\n    )\n\n    cfg = GameLoopStateMachineConfig(\n        lambda_arns=LambdaArns(\n            process_input=args.process_input_arn,\n            update_ai=args.update_ai_arn,\n            physics_step=args.physics_step_arn,\n            render_frame=args.render_frame_arn,\n            persist_state=args.persist_state_arn,\n        ),\n        role_arn=args.iam_role_arn,\n        state_machine_name=args.state_machine_name,\n        tick_rate_hz=args.tick_rate,\n        output_path=args.output or GameLoopStateMachineConfig.output_path,\n    )\n\n    definition = build_state_machine(cfg)\n    write_asl(cfg, definition)\n\n    if args.deploy:\n        deploy_state_machine(cfg, definition)\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    # Sanity check that AWS credentials exist *before* building anything\n    if \"AWS_REGION\" not in os.environ and \"AWS_DEFAULT_REGION\" not in os.environ:\n        os.environ[\"AWS_DEFAULT_REGION\"] = \"us-east-1\"\n        logging.warning(\"AWS region not specified; defaulting to us-east-1\")\n\n    try:\n        main()\n    except KeyboardInterrupt:\n        logging.error(\"Interrupted by user\")\n        sys.exit(130)\n```", "numLines": 324, "startLine": 1, "totalLines": 324}}

     2→"""
     3→ledgerquest.services.game_loop.input_processor
     4→================================================
     5→
     6→The input_processor module is responsible for converting raw
     7→interaction events (typically sent by Web-Socket clients or REST
     8→gateways) into **Command** objects that the rest of the LedgerQuest
     9→game-loop understands.  The processor also performs tenant-aware
    10→validation, idempotency checks, and finally publishes the commands to
    11→an internal EventBridge bus where downstream stateless engines (AI,
    12→physics, etc.) will pick the items up.
    13→
    14→Core Responsibilities
    15→---------------------
    16→1. JSON schema validation & sanitisation of inbound events.
    17→2. Idempotency: guarantee “exactly-once” semantics using a lightweight
    18→   DynamoDB token table.
    19→3. Command translation (Command Pattern).
    20→4. Emission of validated commands to AWS EventBridge with the proper
    21→   routing keys so that subscriber Lambdas / Step-Function workflows
    22→   can continue the game loop.
    23→
    24→The module purposefully keeps all AWS calls small and synchronous
    25→because the Lambda runtime is expected to finish within <50 ms most of
    26→the time.
    27→"""
    28→
    29→from __future__ import annotations
    30→
    31→import hashlib
    32→import json
    33→import logging
    34→import os
    35→from dataclasses import dataclass
    36→from enum import Enum
    37→from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional, Sequence, Tuple
    38→
    39→import boto3
    40→from botocore.client import BaseClient
    41→from botocore.exceptions import ClientError
    42→from jsonschema import ValidationError, validate
    43→from typing_extensions import TypedDict
    44→
    45→__all__ = ["InputProcessor", "InputValidationError", "Command", "InputType"]
    46→
    47→###############################################################################
    48→# Logging configuration
    49→###############################################################################
    50→
    51→LOG_LEVEL = os.getenv("LEDGERQUEST_LOG_LEVEL", "INFO").upper()
    52→logging.basicConfig(level=LOG_LEVEL)
    53→logger = logging.getLogger("ledgerquest.input_processor")
    54→
    55→
    56→###############################################################################
    57→# Exceptions
    58→###############################################################################
    59→
    60→
    61→class InputValidationError(RuntimeError):
    62→    """Raised when an inbound event fails schema validation."""
    63→
    64→    def __init__(self, message: str, *, event: Optional[dict] = None) -> None:
    65→        super().__init__(message)
    66→        self.event = event or {}
    67→
    68→
    69→class DuplicateEventError(RuntimeError):
    70→    """Raised when an event with the same `interaction_id` was processed."""
    71→
    72→
    73→###############################################################################
    74→# Data Model
    75→###############################################################################
    76→
    77→
    78→class InputType(str, Enum):
    79→    """Allowed user input types."""
    80→
    81→    MOVE = "MOVE"
    82→    CAST_SKILL = "CAST_SKILL"
    83→    CHAT = "CHAT"
    84→    PAUSE = "PAUSE"
    85→
    86→
    87→@dataclass(frozen=True)
    88→class Command:
    89→    """
    90→    Canonical command object understood by the lower-level game systems.
    91→
    92→    A command is immutable and is *always* emitted onto EventBridge, from
    93→    where specialised engines pick them up.
    94→    """
    95→
    96→    tenant_id: str
    97→    game_id: str
    98→    player_id: str
    99→    command: InputType
   100→    payload: Dict[str, Any]
   101→    interaction_id: str  # used for idempotency
   102→
   103→
   104→###############################################################################
   105→# AWS resource helpers
   106→###############################################################################
   107→
   108→_DDB_TABLE = os.getenv("LEDGERQUEST_IDEMPOTENCY_TABLE", "ledgerquest-idempotency")
   109→_EVENT_BUS_ARN = os.getenv("LEDGERQUEST_EVENT_BUS_ARN")  # mandatory in prod
   110→
   111→
   112→def _create_ddb_client() -> BaseClient:
   113→    return boto3.client("dynamodb", region_name=os.getenv("AWS_REGION", "us-east-1"))
   114→
   115→
   116→def _create_eventbridge_client() -> BaseClient:
   117→    return boto3.client("events", region_name=os.getenv("AWS_REGION", "us-east-1"))
   118→
   119→
   120→###############################################################################
   121→# JSON Schemas for validation
   122→###############################################################################
   123→
   124→_BASE_SCHEMA: Dict[str, Any] = {
   125→    "type": "object",
   126→    "required": [
   127→        "tenant_id",
   128→        "game_id",
   129→        "player_id",
   130→        "interaction_id",
   131→        "input_type",
   132→        "payload",
   133→    ],
   134→    "properties": {
   135→        "tenant_id": {"type": "string", "minLength": 1},
   136→        "game_id": {"type": "string", "minLength": 1},
   137→        "player_id": {"type": "string", "minLength": 1},
   138→        "interaction_id": {"type": "string", "minLength": 1},
   139→        "input_type": {"type": "string", "enum": [i.value for i in InputType]},
   140→        "payload": {"type": "object"},
   141→    },
   142→    "additionalProperties": False,
   143→}
   144→
   145→###############################################################################
   146→# Public Processor
   147→###############################################################################
   148→
   149→
   150→class InputProcessor:
   151→    """
   152→    Convert & forward raw front-end interaction events to game-loop
   153→    commands.
   154→
   155→    Real-world production considerations implemented:
   156→    • **Validation** via JSON-Schema.
   157→    • **Idempotency** via a DynamoDB token table.
   158→    • **Observability** with structured logging (tenant-aware).
   159→    """
   160→
   161→    def __init__(
   162→        self,
   163→        *,
   164→        ddb_client: Optional[BaseClient] = None,
   165→        eventbridge_client: Optional[BaseClient] = None,
   166→        schema: Dict[str, Any] = _BASE_SCHEMA,
   167→        logger_: logging.Logger = logger,
   168→    ) -> None:
   169→        self.ddb = ddb_client or _create_ddb_client()
   170→        self.eb = eventbridge_client or _create_eventbridge_client()
   171→        self.schema = schema
   172→        self.log = logger_
   173→
   174→        if not _EVENT_BUS_ARN:
   175→            raise EnvironmentError(
   176→                "Environment variable LEDGERQUEST_EVENT_BUS_ARN must be set."
   177→            )
   178→
   179→    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   180→    # Public API
   181→    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   182→
   183→    def handle_event(self, event: Dict[str, Any]) -> Command:
   184→        """
   185→        Full processing pipeline for a single inbound event.
   186→
   187→        Parameters
   188→        ----------
   189→        event:
   190→            The raw event (dict) sent by API Gateway or another
   191→            upstream component.
   192→
   193→        Returns
   194→        -------
   195→        Command
   196→            The Command object that was dispatched.
   197→
   198→        Raises
   199→        ------
   200→        InputValidationError

   234→    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #

     2→"""
     3→ledgerquest.services.game_loop.state_committer
     4→---------------------------------------------
     5→
     6→The *state_committer* is the final step in a single game-loop iteration
     7→orchestrated by AWS Step Functions.  It receives a collection of entity /
     8→component deltas that were produced by the simulation worker and attempts to
     9→commit them to the authoritative data-store while enforcing:
    10→
    11→* Multi-tenant isolation (tenant_id partition key)
    12→* Optimistic concurrency (per-session frame counters)
    13→* Size limits of DynamoDB items (≈ 400 KB)
    14→* Audit, metering, and diagnostic logging
    15→
    16→If the payload is too large for DynamoDB, it is transparently off-loaded to
    17→Amazon S3 and only a pointer is stored in the main table.
    18→
    19→Because every Lambda invocation must be stateless, **StateCommitter** receives
    20→its dependencies (boto3 clients, loggers, …) via *constructor injection* to make
    21→the code testable and to enable local / on-prem adapters.
    22→
    23→Typical usage
    24→~~~~~~~~~~~~~
    25→
    26→    committer = StateCommitter(
    27→        ddb_table_name=os.environ["GAME_STATE_TABLE"],
    28→        s3_bucket_name=os.environ["GAME_STATE_BUCKET"],
    29→        s3_prefix="session-frames/",
    30→    )
    31→
    32→    result = committer.commit_state(
    33→        tenant_id=event["tenant_id"],
    34→        session_id=event["session_id"],
    35→        frame_id=event["frame_id"],
    36→        expected_previous_frame_id=event["expected_previous_frame_id"],
    37→        entity_changes=event["changes"],
    38→    )
    39→
    40→"""
    41→from __future__ import annotations
    42→
    43→import gzip
    44→import json
    45→import logging
    46→import os
    47→import sys
    48→import time
    49→import uuid
    50→from dataclasses import asdict, dataclass
    51→from datetime import datetime
    52→from io import BytesIO
    53→from typing import Any, Dict, List, Mapping, MutableMapping, Optional, Sequence
    54→
    55→import boto3
    56→from botocore.client import BaseClient
    57→from botocore.exceptions import BotoCoreError, ClientError
    58→
    59→__all__ = [
    60→    "StateCommitter",
    61→    "EntityChange",
    62→    "StateCommitError",
    63→    "ConcurrencyConflictError",
    64→    "StorageError",
    65→]
    66→
    67→LOGGER = logging.getLogger(__name__)
    68→LOGGER.setLevel(logging.INFO)
    69→
    70→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
    71→# Constants
    72→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
    73→
    74→MAX_DDB_ITEM_SIZE = 400 * 1024  # Hard DynamoDB limit (400 KB)
    75→GZIP_COMPRESSION_LEVEL = 6       # Reasonable trade-off speed vs. ratio
    76→DEFAULT_S3_STORAGE_CLASS = "STANDARD_IA"  # Cheaper but still fast access
    77→
    78→
    79→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
    80→# Exceptions
    81→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
    82→
    83→
    84→class StateCommitError(RuntimeError):
    85→    """Base-class for commit failures."""
    86→
    87→
    88→class ConcurrencyConflictError(StateCommitError):
    89→    """Raised when optimistic concurrency protection fails."""
    90→
    91→
    92→class StorageError(StateCommitError):
    93→    """Raised when S3 or DynamoDB operations fail for non-concurrency reasons."""
    94→
    95→
    96→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
    97→# Data Model
    98→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
    99→
   100→
   101→@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
   102→class EntityChange:  # noqa: D401
   103→    """Represents a single entity/component mutation.
   104→
   105→    Attributes
   106→    ----------
   107→    entity_id
   108→        The canonical UUID of the entity.
   109→    component
   110→        The component name (e.g., 'Transform', 'Health', 'AccountBalance').
   111→    operation
   112→        'PUT', 'DELETE', or 'UPDATE'.
   113→    payload
   114→        Arbitrary JSON-serialisable structure containing the component data.
   115→    version
   116→        Client-supplied optimistic version of the component (optional).
   117→    """
   118→
   119→    entity_id: str
   120→    component: str
   121→    operation: str
   122→    payload: Mapping[str, Any]
   123→    version: Optional[int] = None
   124→
   125→    def to_dict(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
   126→        return asdict(self)
   127→
   128→
   129→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   130→# Helpers
   131→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   132→
   133→
   134→def _utc_now_rfc3339() -> str:
   135→    """Return the current UTC timestamp in RFC 3339 format."""
   136→    return datetime.utcnow().isoformat(timespec="milliseconds") + "Z"
   137→
   138→
   139→def _estimate_item_size(item: Mapping[str, Any]) -> int:
   140→    """Approximate size of a DynamoDB item after JSON serialisation."""
   141→    # NOTE: DynamoDB binary encoding is different, but JSON is a good proxy.
   142→    return len(json.dumps(item, separators=(",", ":")).encode("utf-8"))
   143→
   144→
   145→def _gzip_compress(data: bytes, level: int = GZIP_COMPRESSION_LEVEL) -> bytes:
   146→    """Compress *data* using GZIP."""
   147→    buffer = BytesIO()
   148→    with gzip.GzipFile(fileobj=buffer, mode="wb", compresslevel=level) as gz:
   149→        gz.write(data)
   150→    return buffer.getvalue()
   151→
   152→
   153→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   154→# Core Service
   155→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   156→
   157→
   158→class StateCommitter:  # noqa: D401
   159→    """Persist simulation results whilst enforcing concurrency & compliance."""
   160→
   161→    def __init__(
