It is designed to\nbe triggered by an AWS Step-Functions `Task` state (or EventBridge\nScheduler), and persists the updated entity components back to\nDynamoDB.  The routine is intentionally lightweight—only the minimal\nmath required for business-grade “serious games”.  Heavy GPU/CPU\nworkloads should still be off-loaded to dedicated workers (e.g.\nFargate).\n\nEnvironment variables\n---------------------\nENTITIES_TABLE_NAME   : DynamoDB table that stores entity component state\nEVENT_BUS_NAME        : Optional ‑ EventBridge bus to publish collision events\nLOG_LEVEL             : stdlib logging level (default=INFO)\n\nInput event contract\n--------------------\n{\n    \"scene_id\": \"<uuid>\",\n    \"delta_time\": 0.016  # seconds since previous physics frame\n}\n\nOutput contract (Step-Functions result)\n---------------------------------------\n{\n    \"scene_id\": \"<uuid>\",\n    \"updated_entities\": 42,\n    \"collisions\": 3,\n    \"duration_ms\": 12.34\n}\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport json\nimport logging\nimport os\nimport time\nimport uuid\nfrom dataclasses import asdict, dataclass, field\nfrom decimal import Decimal\nfrom typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional, Tuple\n\nimport boto3\nfrom botocore.exceptions import ClientError\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------#\n# Logging setup\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------#\nLOG_LEVEL = os.getenv(\"LOG_LEVEL\", \"INFO\").upper()\nlogging.basicConfig(level=LOG_LEVEL)\nlogger = logging.getLogger(__name__)\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------#\n# Constants & Config\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------#\nGRAVITY: float = 9.81  # m/s^2, positive downwards\nMAX_BATCH_WRITE: int = 25  # DynamoDB service limit\nDYNAMO_FLOAT_PRECISION = 8  # decimal places for serialization\n\n_ENTITIES_TABLE_NAME = os.getenv(\"ENTITIES_TABLE_NAME\")\nif not _ENTITIES_TABLE_NAME:\n    logger.error(\"Missing required env var ENTITIES_TABLE_NAME\")\n    raise RuntimeError(\"ENTITIES_TABLE_NAME environment variable not set\")\n\n_EVENT_BUS_NAME = os.getenv(\"EVENT_BUS_NAME\")  # optional\n\n_dynamo = boto3.resource(\"dynamodb\")\n_entities_table = _dynamo.Table(_ENTITIES_TABLE_NAME)\n\n_events_client = boto3.client(\"events\") if _EVENT_BUS_NAME else None\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------#\n# Helper datatypes\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------#\n@dataclass\nclass Vec2:\n    \"\"\"Simple 2-D vector for physics calculations.\"\"\"\n    x: float\n    y: float\n\n    def __add__(self, other: \"Vec2\") -> \"Vec2\":\n        return Vec2(self.x + other.x, self.y + other.y)\n\n    def __mul__(self, scalar: float) -> \"Vec2\":\n        return Vec2(self.x * scalar, self.y * scalar)\n\n    def copy(self) -> \"Vec2\":\n        return Vec2(self.x, self.y)\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass AABB:\n    \"\"\"Axis-Aligned Bounding Box used for naïve collision checks.\"\"\"\n    min_x: float\n    min_y: float\n    max_x: float\n    max_y: float\n\n    def intersects(self, other: \"AABB\") -> bool:\n        return (\n            self.max_x >= other.min_x\n            and self.min_x <= other.max_x\n            and self.max_y >= other.min_y\n            and self.min_y <= other.max_y\n        )\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass EntityPhysicsState:\n    \"\"\"Subset of a full entity component specific to physics.\"\"\"\n    entity_id: str\n    scene_id: str\n    pos: Vec2\n    vel: Vec2\n    size: Vec2  # half-width / half-height (extents)\n    mass: float\n    is_static: bool = False\n    version: int = 0  # optimistic-locking version\n\n    # runtime-only computed field (not persisted)\n    _aabb: AABB = field(init=False, repr=False)\n\n    def __post_init__(self) -> None:\n        self.compute_aabb()\n\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------#\n    # Behaviour helpers\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------#\n    def compute_aabb(self) -> None:\n        self._aabb = AABB(\n            self.pos.x - self.size.x,\n            self.pos.y - self.size.y,\n            self.pos.x + self.size.x,\n            self.pos.y + self.size.y,\n        )\n\n    def apply_gravity(self, delta: float) -> None:\n        if not self.is_static:\n            self.vel.y += GRAVITY * delta\n\n    def integrate(self, delta: float) -> None:\n        if self.is_static:\n            return\n        self.pos = self.pos + self.vel * delta\n        self.compute_aabb()\n\n    def bounce(self, normal: Tuple[float, float]) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Very simple reflection model with full energy loss on normal axis.\"\"\"\n        nx, ny = normal\n        if nx != 0:\n            self.vel.x = -self.vel.x * 0.5  # some damping\n        if ny != 0:\n            self.vel.y = -self.vel.y * 0.5\n        self.compute_aabb()\n\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------#\n    # (De)serialization helpers\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------#\n    def to_dynamo_item(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n        \"\"\"Serialize to a shape that can be put into DynamoDB.\"\"\"\n        return {\n            \"pk\": f\"scene#{self.scene_id}\",\n            \"sk\": f\"ent#{self.entity_id}\",\n            \"component\": \"physics\",\n            \"pos\": {\n                \"x\": _to_dynamo_float(self.pos.x),\n                \"y\": _to_dynamo_float(self.pos.y),\n            },\n            \"vel\": {\n                \"x\": _to_dynamo_float(self.vel.x),\n                \"y\": _to_dynamo_float(self.vel.y),\n            },\n            \"size\": {\n                \"x\": _to_dynamo_float(self.size.x),\n                \"y\": _to_dynamo_float(self.size.y),\n            },\n            \"mass\": _to_dynamo_float(self.mass),\n            \"is_static\": self.is_static,\n            \"version\": self.version + 1,  # increment\n            \"updated_at\": int(time.time() * 1000),\n        }\n\n    @classmethod\n    def from_dynamo_item(cls, item: Dict[str, Any]) -> \"EntityPhysicsState\":\n        return cls(\n            entity_id=item[\"sk\"].split(\"#\")[1],\n            scene_id=item[\"pk\"].split(\"#\")[1],\n            pos=Vec2(\n                float(item[\"pos\"][\"x\"]),\n                float(item[\"pos\"][\"y\"]),\n            ),\n            vel=Vec2(\n                float(item[\"vel\"][\"x\"]),\n                float(item[\"vel\"][\"y\"]),\n            ),\n            size=Vec2(\n                float(item[\"size\"][\"x\"]),\n                float(item[\"size\"][\"y\"]),\n            ),\n            mass=float(item[\"mass\"]),\n            is_static=item.get(\"is_static\", False),\n            version=int(item.get(\"version\", 0)),\n        )\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------#\n# Physics System\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------#\nclass PhysicsUpdater:\n    \"\"\"Encapsulates physics simulation for a single time-step.\"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, scene_id: str, delta_time: float) -> None:\n        self.scene_id = scene_id\n        self.delta = max(delta_time, 0.001)  # clamp to avoid div-by-zero\n        self.entities: List[EntityPhysicsState] = []\n        self.collisions: List[Tuple[str, str]] = []\n\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------#\n    # Public helpers\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------#\n    def step(self) -> None:\n        self._load_entities()\n        logger.debug(\"Loaded %d physics entities\", len(self.entities))\n        self._apply_forces()\n        self._integrate()\n        self._detect_and_resolve_collisions()\n        self._persist()\n        self._emit_collision_events()\n\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------#\n    # Internal helpers\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------#\n    def _load_entities(self) -> None:\n        try:\n            response = _entities_table.query(\n                KeyConditionExpression=\"pk = :pk AND begins_with(sk, :sk)\",\n                ExpressionAttributeValues={\n                    \":pk\": f\"scene#{self.scene_id}\",\n                    \":sk\": \"ent#\",\n                },\n                FilterExpression=\"component = :physics\",\n                ExpressionAttributeNames=None,\n                ExpressionAttributeValues2={\":physics\": \"physics\"},\n            )\n        except TypeError:\n            # boto3's query() unfortunately won't allow mixing FilterExpression\n            # with ExpressionAttributeValues2; fall back to scan().  It's still\n            # acceptable for small partitions (< ~100 items).\n            logger.debug(\"Falling back to full scan for scene_id=%s\", self.scene_id)\n            response = _entities_table.scan(\n                FilterExpression=\"pk = :pk AND component = :physics\",\n                ExpressionAttributeValues={\n                    \":pk\": f\"scene#{self.scene_id}\",\n                    \":physics\": \"physics\",\n                }\n            )\n\n        items = response.get(\"Items\", [])\n        self.entities = [EntityPhysicsState.from_dynamo_item(i) for i in items]\n\n    def _apply_forces(self) -> None:\n        for ent in self.entities:\n            ent.apply_gravity(self.delta)\n\n    def _integrate(self) -> None:\n        for ent in self.entities:\n            ent.integrate(self.delta)\n\n    def _detect_and_resolve_collisions(self) -> None:\n        # Naïve O(N^2) for demo purposes; optimise using spatial hashing if needed\n        count = len(self.entities)\n        for i in range(count):\n            a = self.entities[i]\n            if a.is_static:\n                continue\n            for j in range(i + 1, count):\n                b = self.entities[j]\n                if a._aabb.intersects(b._aabb):\n                    # Simple symmetric collision response\n                    normal = self._compute_collision_normal(a, b)\n                    a.bounce(normal)\n                    if not b.is_static:\n                        b.bounce((-normal[0], -normal[1]))\n                    self.collisions.append((a.entity_id, b.entity_id))\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def _compute_collision_normal(a: EntityPhysicsState, b: EntityPhysicsState) -> Tuple[float, float]:\n        # Determine which axis penetration is smaller\n        dx = (b.pos.x - a.pos.x)\n        dy = (b.pos.y - a.pos.y)\n        abs_dx, abs_dy = abs(dx), abs(dy)\n        if abs_dx > abs_dy:\n            # collision comes from left or right\n            return (1 if dx > 0 else -1, 0)\n        else:\n            return (0, 1 if dy > 0 else -1)\n\n    def _persist(self) -> None:\n        # Write in batches of 25\n        batch: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []\n        for ent in self.entities:\n            batch.append({\"PutRequest\": {\"Item\": ent.to_dynamo_item()}})\n            if len(batch) == MAX_BATCH_WRITE:\n                self._flush_batch(batch)\n                batch.clear()\n        if batch:\n            self._flush_batch(batch)\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def _flush_batch(batch: List[Dict[str, Any]]) -> None:\n        try:\n            response = _entities_table.meta.client.batch_write_item(\n                RequestItems={_ENTITIES_TABLE_NAME: batch}\n            )\n            unprocessed = response.get(\"UnprocessedItems\", {})\n            if unprocessed:\n                logger.warning(\"Retrying %d unprocessed items\", len(unprocessed))\n                # simple naive retry once\n                _entities_table.meta.client.batch_write_item(\n                    RequestItems=unprocessed\n                )\n        except ClientError as exc:\n            logger.error(\"DynamoDB batch_write_item failed: %s\", exc, exc_info=True)\n            raise\n\n    def _emit_collision_events(self) -> None:\n        if not self.collisions or _events_client is None:\n            return\n\n        entries = []\n        for a_id, b_id in self.collisions[:10]:  # limit to 10 per invocation\n            entries.append(\n                {\n                    \"Source\": \"ledgerquest.physics\",\n                    \"DetailType\": \"COLLISION\",\n                    \"EventBusName\": _EVENT_BUS_NAME,\n                    \"Time\": int(time.time() * 1000),\n                    \"Detail\": json.dumps(\n                        {\n                            \"scene_id\": self.scene_id,\n                            \"a\": a_id,\n                            \"b\": b_id,\n                        }\n                    ),\n                }\n            )\n\n        # EventBridge allows max 10 entries / put_events call\n        try:\n            _events_client.put_events(Entries=entries)\n        except ClientError as exc:\n            logger.error(\"Failed to publish collision events: %s\", exc, exc_info=True)\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------#\n# Lambda entrypoint\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------#\ndef lambda_handler(event: Dict[str, Any], _context: Any) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n    \"\"\"\n    AWS Lambda handler.  Expects a Step-Function / EventBridge payload.\n\n    Parameter\n    ---------\n    event : dict\n        {\n            \"scene_id\": \"<uuid>\",\n            \"delta_time\": 0.016\n        }\n\n    Returns\n    -------\n    dict  # same shape for downstream steps\n    \"\"\"\n    t0 = time.perf_counter()\n    try:\n        scene_id = str(event[\"scene_id\"])\n        delta = float(event.get(\"delta_time\", 0.016))\n    except (KeyError, ValueError, TypeError) as exc:\n        logger.error(\"Bad input event: %s\", exc, exc_info=True)\n        raise\n\n    logger.info(\"Physics step start scene=%s  Δt=%.4fs\", scene_id, delta)\n\n    simulator = PhysicsUpdater(scene_id, delta)\n    simulator.step()\n\n    duration_ms = (time.perf_counter() - t0) * 1000.0\n    logger.info(\n        \"Physics step complete scene=%s entities=%d collisions=%d (%.2fms)\",\n        scene_id,\n        len(simulator.entities),\n        len(simulator.collisions),\n        duration_ms,\n    )\n\n    return {\n        \"scene_id\": scene_id,\n        \"updated_entities\": len(simulator.entities),\n        \"collisions\": len(simulator.collisions),\n        \"duration_ms\": round(duration_ms, 2),\n    }\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------#\n# Utility helpers\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------#\ndef _to_dynamo_float(value: float) -> Decimal:\n    \"\"\"\n    DynamoDB does not support IEEE-754 floats; we use `Decimal` with a fixed\n    precision to maintain deterministic JSON hashing across our pipeline.\n    \"\"\"\n    fmt = f\"{{:.{DYNAMO_FLOAT_PRECISION}f}}\"\n    return Decimal(fmt.format(value))\n", "numLines": 421, "startLine": 1, "totalLines": 421}}

     2→"""
     3→ledgerquest.services.game_loop.ai_updater
     4→========================================
     5→
     6→AWS Lambda entry-point responsible for advancing AI behaviour for a single
     7→game tick.  The function is invoked by an AWS Step-Functions state machine
     8→that orchestrates the stateless “game-loop” for LedgerQuest Engine.
     9→
    10→The AI updater performs the following high-level steps:
    11→
    12→1.  Input validation & context bootstrap (tenant, session, tick, etc.)
    13→2.  Pull the set of ECS entities that own an ``AIComponent`` and require an
    14→    update this tick.
    15→3.  Evaluate each entity’s Behaviour-Tree (BT) against the current world
    16→    state, producing one or more commands.
    17→4.  Persist the updated component state and publish resulting commands to an
    18→    EventBridge bus so that downstream state mutators (e.g., physics
    19→    simulator) can consume them.
    20→
    21→The module purposefully keeps all objects in memory during execution to
    22→honour the Lambda “stateless but warm container” paradigm—no globals are
    23→written to. Behaviour-Tree definitions are cached across invocations via the
    24→*execution context* (module-level) to minimise S3 latency.
    25→
    26→Author: LedgerQuest Team
    27→"""
    28→from __future__ import annotations
    29→
    30→import json
    31→import logging
    32→import os
    33→import random
    34→import time
    35→from dataclasses import dataclass
    36→from typing import Any, Dict, List, Protocol
    37→
    38→import boto3
    39→from botocore.exceptions import ClientError
    40→from botocore.config import Config
    41→
    42→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
    43→# Logging
    44→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
    45→LOG_LEVEL = os.getenv("LEDGERQUEST_LOG_LEVEL", "INFO").upper()
    46→logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
    47→logger.setLevel(LOG_LEVEL)
    48→
    49→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
    50→# AWS Clients (re-use across warm invocations)
    51→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
    52→_BOTO_CONFIG = Config(retries={"max_attempts": 5, "mode": "adaptive"})
    53→_dynamodb = boto3.resource("dynamodb", config=_BOTO_CONFIG)
    54→_s3 = boto3.client("s3", config=_BOTO_CONFIG)
    55→_eventbridge = boto3.client("events", config=_BOTO_CONFIG)
    56→
    57→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
    58→# Environment
    59→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
    60→DDB_ENTITY_TABLE = os.getenv("LEDGERQUEST_ENTITY_TABLE", "ledgerquest_entities")
    61→DDB_COMPONENT_GSI = os.getenv("LEDGERQUEST_COMPONENT_GSI", "components-index")
    62→BT_S3_BUCKET = os.getenv("LEDGERQUEST_BT_BUCKET", "ledgerquest-behaviour-trees")
    63→EVENT_BUS = os.getenv("LEDGERQUEST_EVENT_BUS", "ledgerquest-game-events")
    64→MAX_BATCH_WRITE = 25  # DynamoDB maximum for batch_write_item
    65→
    66→
    67→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
    68→# ECS & Behaviour-Tree Models
    69→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
    70→@dataclass
    71→class EntityState:
    72→    """Simplified ECS entity snapshot required for AI evaluation."""
    73→    entity_id: str
    74→    session_id: str
    75→    tenant_id: str
    76→    position: Dict[str, float]
    77→    ai_state: Dict[str, Any]  # arbitrary per-entity scratch-pad
    78→
    79→
    80→class Blackboard(dict):
    81→    """Shared memory for BT nodes during a single tick."""
    82→
    83→
    84→class BTNode(Protocol):
    85→    """Behaviour-Tree node protocol."""
    86→
    87→    def tick(self, state: EntityState, blackboard: Blackboard) -> bool: ...
    88→
    89→
    90→class Selector:
    91→    """Returns `True` when the first child returns `True`."""
    92→
    93→    def __init__(self, *children: BTNode) -> None:
    94→        self._children = children
    95→
    96→    def tick(self, state: EntityState, blackboard: Blackboard) -> bool:
    97→        for child in self._children:
    98→            if child.tick(state, blackboard):
    99→                return True
   100→        return False
   101→
   102→
   103→class Sequence:
   104→    """Returns `True` when *all* children return `True`."""
   105→
   106→    def __init__(self, *children: BTNode) -> None:
   107→        self._children = children
   108→
   109→    def tick(self, state: EntityState, blackboard: Blackboard) -> bool:
   110→        for child in self._children:
   111→            if not child.tick(state, blackboard):
   112→                return False
   113→        return True
   114→
   115→
   116→class ConditionNode:
   117→    """Evaluates a predicate stored in BT specification."""
   118→
   119→    def __init__(self, predicate: str) -> None:
   120→        self._predicate = predicate
   121→
   122→    def tick(self, state: EntityState, blackboard: Blackboard) -> bool:
   123→        # In real world, predicate would be compiled Python or DSL.
   124→        # For demo, support a few hard-coded predicates.
   125→        if self._predicate == "is_player_nearby":
   126→            # Randomised example
   127→            is_near = random.random() < 0.3
   128→            logger.debug("Predicate is_player_nearby -> %s", is_near)
   129→            return is_near
   130→        if self._predicate == "low_health":
   131→            # Suppose health sits on blackboard
   132→            low = blackboard.get("health", 100) < 25
   133→            logger.debug("Predicate low_health -> %s", low)
   134→            return low
   135→        logger.warning("Unknown predicate %s, defaulting to False", self._predicate)
   136→        return False
   137→
   138→
   139→class ActionNode:
   140→    """Produces commands for the entity; always returns `True`."""
   141→
   142→    def __init__(self, command_type: str, **params: Any) -> None:
   143→        self._command_type = command_type
   144→        self._params = params
   145→
   146→    def tick(self, state: EntityState, blackboard: Blackboard) -> bool:
   147→        # Store commands on the blackboard; they will be flushed later.
   148→        commands: List[Dict[str, Any]] = blackboard.setdefault("commands", [])
   149→        commands.append(
   150→            {
   151→                "type": self._command_type,
   152→                "entity_id": state.entity_id,
   153→                "params": self._params,
   154→            }
   155→        )
   156→        logger.debug(
   157→            "Queued command %s for entity %s", self._command_type, state.entity_id
   158→        )
   159→        return True
   160→
   161→
   162→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   163→# Behaviour-Tree Registry
   164→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   165→_BT_CACHE: Dict[str, BTNode] = {}
   166→
   167→
   168→def _load_bt_from_s3(key: str) -> BTNode:
   169→    """Load & parse BT JSON from S3, then cache the compiled tree."""
   170→    if key in _BT_CACHE:
   171→        return _BT_CACHE[key]
   172→
   173→    try:
   174→        response = _s3.get_object(Bucket=BT_S3_BUCKET, Key=key)
   175→        definition = json.loads(response["Body"].read())
   176→        root = _compile_bt(definition)
   177→        _BT_CACHE[key] = root
   178→        logger.info("Behaviour-Tree %s loaded and cached", key)
   179→        return root
   180→    except ClientError as exc:
   181→        logger.error("Failed to load BT %s from S3: %s", key, exc)
   182→        raise
   183→
   184→
   185→def _compile_bt(spec: Dict[str, Any]) -> BTNode:
   186→    """Recursively compile a JSON BT specification into runtime objects."""
   187→    n_type = spec["type"]
   188→    if n_type == "selector":
   189→        return Selector(*[_compile_bt(child) for child in spec["children"]])
   190→    if n_type == "sequence":
   191→        return Sequence(*[_compile_bt(child) for child in spec["children"]])
   192→    if n_type == "condition":
   193→        return ConditionNode(spec["predicate"])
   194→    if n_type == "action":
   195→        return ActionNode(spec["command"], **spec.get("params", {}))
   196→    raise ValueError(f"Unknown BT node type: {n_type}")
   197→
   198→
   199→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   200→# DynamoDB Helpers
   201→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   202→def _paginated_scan_entities(
   203→    tenant_id: str, session_id: str, limit: int = 1000
   204→) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
   205→    """Scan the entity table for AI components, respecting GSIs for efficiency."""
   206→    table = _dynamodb.Table(DDB_ENTITY_TABLE)
   207→    start_key = None
   208→    items: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []
   209→
   210→    key_expr = "tenant_id = :tenant AND session_id = :sess"
   211→    filter_expr = "contains(components, :ai)"
   212→    expr_attr_values = {
   213→        ":tenant": tenant_id,
   214→        ":sess": session_id,
   215→        ":ai": "AIComponent",
   216→    }
   217→
   218→    while True:
   219→        resp = table.scan(
   220→            IndexName=DDB_COMPONENT_GSI,
   221→            ExclusiveStartKey=start_key,
   222→            FilterExpression=filter_expr,
   223→            KeyConditionExpression=key_expr,  # type: ignore[arg-type]
   224→            ExpressionAttributeValues=expr_attr_values,
   225→            Limit=limit,
   226→        )
   227→        items.extend(resp.get("Items", []))
   228→        start_key = resp.get("LastEvaluatedKey")
   229→        if not start_key:
   230→            break
   231→    logger.info(
   232→        "Fetched %s AI-enabled entities for tenant=%s session=%s",
   233→        len(items),
   234→        tenant_id,
   235→        session_id,
   236→    )
   237→    return items
   238→
   239→
   240→def _batch_write_with_retries(
   241→    table_name: str, requests: List[Dict[str, Any]], retries: int = 3
   242→) -> None:
   243→    """Write items to DynamoDB with exponential backoff."""
   244→    client = boto3.client("dynamodb", config=_BOTO_CONFIG)
   245→    for attempt in range(1, retries + 1):
   246→        try:
   247→            resp = client.batch_write_item(RequestItems={table_name: requests})
   248→            unprocessed = resp.get("UnprocessedItems", {}).get(table_name, [])
   249→            if unprocessed:
   250→                logger.warning(
   251→                    "Attempt %d: %d items unprocessed, retrying",
   252→                    attempt,
   253→                    len(unprocessed),
   254→                )
   255→                requests = unprocessed
   256→                time.sleep(2 ** attempt)
   257→                continue
   258→            return
   259→        except ClientError as exc:
   260→            logger.error(
   261→                "Error writing to DynamoDB (attempt %d/%d): %s", attempt, retries, exc
   262→            )
   263→            time.sleep(2 ** attempt)
   264→    if requests:
   265→        logger.error("Failed to write some items to DynamoDB after retries: %s", requests)
   266→        raise RuntimeError("DynamoDB batch write failed")
   267→
   268→
   269→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   270→# Lambda Handler
   271→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   272→def lambda_handler(event: Dict[str, Any], context: Any) -> Dict[str, Any]:
   273→    """
   274→    Lambda entry-point.
   275→
   276→    Expected ``event`` shape:
   277→    {
   278→        "tenant_id": "corp-foo",
   279→        "session_id": "sess-uuid",
   280→        "tick": 1024
   281→    }
   282→    """
   283→    tenant_id = event.get("tenant_id")
   284→    session_id = event.get("session_id")
   285→    tick = event.get("tick")
   286→
   287→    if not (tenant_id and session_id and isinstance(tick, int)):
   288→        logger.error("Invalid invocation parameters: %s", event)
   289→        raise ValueError("Missing required parameters")
   290→
   291→    entities_raw = _paginated_scan_entities(tenant_id, session_id)
   292→    entities: List[EntityState] = [
   293→        EntityState(
   294→            entity_id=e["entity_id"],
   295→            session_id=session_id,
   296→            tenant_id=tenant_id,
   297→            position=e.get("position", {"x": 0, "y": 0}),
   298→            ai_state=e.get("components", {}).get("AIComponent", {}),
   299→        )
   300→        for e in entities_raw
   301→    ]
   302→
   303→    ddb_updates: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []
   304→    event_entries: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []
   305→
   306→    for entity in entities:
   307→        try:
   308→            bt_key = entity.ai_state.get("behaviour_tree_key")
   309→            if not bt_key:
   310→                logger.warning("Entity %s missing behaviour_tree_key", entity.entity_id)
   311→                continue
   312→
   313→            blackboard = Blackboard()
   314→
   315→            # Populate blackboard with dynamic values (simplified)
   316→            blackboard["health"] = entity.ai_state.get("health", 100)
   317→
   318→            bt_root = _load_bt_from_s3(bt_key)
   319→            bt_root.tick(entity, blackboard)
   320→
   321→            commands = blackboard.get("commands", [])
   322→            logger.debug(
   323→                "Entity %s produced %d commands", entity.entity_id, len(commands)
   324→            )
   325→
   326→            # Prepare DynamoDB update for the AI scratch-pad
   327→            ddb_updates.append(
   328→                {
   329→                    "Update": {
   330→                        "Key": {"entity_id": {"S": entity.entity_id}},
   331→                        "TableName": DDB_ENTITY_TABLE,
   332→                        "UpdateExpression": "SET components.#ai = :val",
   333→                        "ExpressionAttributeNames": {"#ai": "AIComponent"},
   334→                        "ExpressionAttributeValues": {
   335→                            ":val": {"S": json.dumps(entity.ai_state)}
   336→                        },
   337→                    }
   338→                }
   339→            )
   340→
   341→            # Prepare EventBridge entries
   342→            for cmd in commands:
   343→                event_entries.append(
   344→                    {
   345→                        "Source": "ledgerquest.ai",
   346→                        "DetailType": cmd["type"],
   347→                        "EventBusName": EVENT_BUS,
   348→                        "Detail": json.dumps(
   349→                            {
   350→                                "tenant_id": tenant_id,
   351→                                "session_id": session_id,
   352→                                "tick": tick,
   353→                                **cmd,
   354→                            }
   355→                        ),
   356→                    }
   357→                )
   358→        except Exception as exc:  # pylint: disable=broad-except
   359→            logger.exception("Failed processing entity %s: %s", entity.entity_id, exc)
   360→
   361→    # Commit updates
   362→    if ddb_updates:
   363→        # DynamoDB batch_write_item requires <=25 items
   364→        for i in range(0, len(ddb_updates), MAX_BATCH_WRITE):
   365→            _batch_write_with_retries(
   366→                DDB_ENTITY_TABLE, ddb_updates[i : i + MAX_BATCH_WRITE]
   367→            )
   368→
   369→    if event_entries:
   370→        # EventBridge put_events allows up to 10 entries
   371→        for i in range(0, len(event_entries), 10):
   372→            batch = event_entries[i : i + 10]
   373→            try:
   374→                response = _eventbridge.put_events(Entries=batch)
   375→                failed = response.get("FailedEntryCount", 0)
   376→                if failed:
   377→                    logger.error("%d EventBridge entries failed: %s", failed, response)
   378→            except ClientError as exc:
   379→                logger.error("Error publishing to EventBridge: %s", exc)
   380→
   381→    return {
   382→        "status": "ok",
   383→        "processed_entities": len(entities),
   384→        "commands_emitted": len(event_entries),
   385→        "tick": tick,
   386→    }

1→```python
     2→"""
     3→ledgerquest.engine.core.command
     4→================================
     5→
     6→Infrastructure layer for LedgerQuest’s Command pattern.
     7→
     8→The module provides:
     9→
    10→1. Command – an immutable, serialisable value-object representing an
    11→   intent to change game state.
    12→2. CommandHandler – an abstract base for classes that *execute* a
    13→   Command against the external ECS/DynamoDB/S3 back-end.
    14→3. CommandRegistry – a lightweight service-locator that maps Command
    15→   types to their respective handlers.  Registration happens
    16→   automatically through decorators.
    17→4. CommandBus – a thin façade that receives a Command instance,
    18→   resolves the correct handler, and calls `handle()` with robust
    19→   error-handling and audit logging.
    20→
    21→These building blocks are intentionally framework-agnostic so they can
    22→be used inside AWS Lambda functions, Step-Functions state machines, or
    23→local unit tests with equal ease.
    24→"""
    25→
    26→from __future__ import annotations
    27→
    28→import importlib
    29→import inspect
    30→import json
    31→import logging
    32→import os
    33→import time
    34→import uuid
    35→from abc import ABC, abstractmethod
    36→from dataclasses import asdict, dataclass, field
    37→from datetime import datetime, timezone
    38→from typing import Any, Dict, Generic, Mapping, MutableMapping, Optional, Type, TypeVar, Union
    39→
    40→__all__ = [
    41→    "Command",
    42→    "CommandHandler",
    43→    "CommandRegistry",
    44→    "CommandBus",
    45→    "CommandError",
    46→    "CommandValidationError",
    47→    "CommandHandlerNotFound",
    48→    "ECSGateway",
    49→]
    50→
    51→log = logging.getLogger("ledgerquest.engine.command")
    52→
    53→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
    54→#                                Exceptions                                   #
    55→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
    56→
    57→
    58→class CommandError(RuntimeError):
    59→    """Base-class for all Command-related exceptions."""
    60→
    61→
    62→class CommandValidationError(CommandError):
    63→    """Raised when a Command fails semantic or structural validation."""
    64→
    65→
    66→class CommandHandlerNotFound(CommandError):
    67→    """Raised when the CommandBus cannot locate a handler for the given Command."""
    68→
    69→
    70→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
    71→#                   Core Domain – Command & Handler                           #
    72→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
    73→
    74→T_Command = TypeVar("T_Command", bound="Command")
    75→
    76→
    77→@dataclass(frozen=True, kw_only=True)
    78→class Command(ABC):
    79→    """
    80→    An immutable value-object that represents a request to mutate game
    81→    state.  Concrete command classes should inherit from this base
    82→    class and declare their own business fields:
    83→
    84→        @command
    85→        @dataclass(frozen=True, kw_only=True)
    86→        class SpawnNPC(Command):
    87→            npc_type: str
    88→            position: Vec3
    89→            tenant_id: str
    90→            user_id: str
    91→    """
    92→
    93→    tenant_id: str
    94→    user_id: str
    95→    correlation_id: Optional[str] = None
    96→
    97→    # Metadata automatically injected for traceability.
    98→    command_id: str = field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4()))
    99→    created_at: float = field(default_factory=lambda: time.time())
   100→
   101→    # ------------------------- Convenience helpers ------------------------ #
   102→
   103→    def iso_timestamp(self) -> str:
   104→        return (
   105→            datetime.fromtimestamp(self.created_at, tz=timezone.utc)
   106→            .replace(microsecond=0)
   107→            .isoformat()
   108→        )
   109→
   110→    def as_dict(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
   111→        return asdict(self)
   112→
   113→    def to_json(self) -> str:

   153→class CommandHandler(Generic[T_Command], ABC):

   185→        responses or Step-Functions state outputs.

   189→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   190→#               Service-Locator – Register & Discover handlers                #
   191→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   192→
   193→
   194→class CommandRegistry:
   195→    """
   196→    A global registry mapping Command subclasses to their respective
   197→    *handler* classes.  The registry is intentionally simple and
   198→    depends on explicit code-based registration to avoid import-time

   203→
   204→    # ------------------------------ API ----------------------------------- #
   205→
   206→    @classmethod
   207→    def register(
   208→        cls, *, command_cls: Type[Command], handler_cls: Type[CommandHandler]
   209→    ) -> None:
   210→        log.debug(
   211→            "Registering Command handler – %s -> %s",
   212→            command_cls.__qualname__,
   213→            handler_cls.__qualname__,
   214→        )
   215→        if command_cls in cls._command_to_handler:
   216→            existing = cls._command_to_handler[command_cls]

   219→                f"{existing.__qualname__} vs {handler_cls.__qualname__}"

   223→    @classmethod
   224→    def handler_for(cls, command_cls: Type[T_Command]) -> Optional[Type[CommandHandler[T_Command]]]:
   225→        return cls._command_to_handler.get(command_cls)
   226→

   247→    # --------------------------- Discovery -------------------------------- #

   266→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #

   342→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   343→#                       Sugar – user-friendly decorator                       #
   344→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #

   375→    class InMemoryECS(ECSGateway):
   376→        """Simple in-memory ECS for smoke-tests."""

   378→        def __init__(self) -> None:
   379→            self._store: Dict[str, Dict[str, Mapping[str, Any]]] = {}
   380→
   381→        def apply_component_patch(

   385→            payload: Mapping[str, Any],

   387→            tenant_id: str,
   388→            user_id: str,
   389→        ) -> None:
   390→            entity = self._store.setdefault(entity_id, {})
   391→            comp = dict(entity.get(component_name, {}))
   392→            comp.update(payload)
   393→            entity[component_name] = comp
   394→            log.debug(

   396→            )

   403→    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   404→    #                  Example domain-specific Command                       #
   405→    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   406→
   407→    @dataclass(frozen=True, kw_only=True)
   408→    class SetHealth(Command):
   409→        entity_id: str
   410→        new_hp: int
   411→
   412→    @command_handler(SetHealth)

   424→                payload={"hp": command.new_hp},

   434→    ecs = InMemoryECS()

   437→    result = bus.dispatch(

Only executed during `python -m ledgerquest.engine.core.command`.\n    from typing import Mapping\n\n    class InMemoryECS(ECSGateway):\n        \"\"\"Simple in-memory ECS for smoke-tests.\"\"\"\n\n        def __init__(self) -> None:\n            self._store: Dict[str, Dict[str, Mapping[str, Any]]] = {}\n\n        def apply_component_patch(\n            self,\n            entity_id: str,\n            component_name: str,\n            payload: Mapping[str, Any],\n            *,\n            tenant_id: str,\n            user_id: str,\n        ) -> None:\n            entity = self._store.setdefault(entity_id, {})\n            comp = dict(entity.get(component_name, {}))\n            comp.update(payload)\n            entity[component_name] = comp\n            log.debug(\n                \"[TEST] %s/%s – Patched %s: %s\", tenant_id, entity_id, component_name, payload\n            )\n\n        def fetch_component(\n            self, entity_id: str, component_name: str, *, tenant_id: str\n        ) -> Mapping[str, Any]:\n            return self._store.get(entity_id, {}).get(component_name, {})\n\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    #                  Example domain-specific Command                       #\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n\n    @dataclass(frozen=True, kw_only=True)\n    class SetHealth(Command):\n        entity_id: str\n        new_hp: int\n\n    @command_handler(SetHealth)\n    class SetHealthHandler(CommandHandler[SetHealth]):\n        command_cls = SetHealth\n\n        def validate(self, command: SetHealth) -> None:  # noqa: D401\n            if command.new_hp < 0:\n                raise CommandValidationError(\"new_hp must be >= 0\")\n\n        def handle(self, command: SetHealth) -> Dict[str, Any]:  # noqa: D401\n            self._ecs_gateway.apply_component_patch(\n                command.entity_id,\n                component_name=\"Health\",\n                payload={\"hp\": command.new_hp},\n                tenant_id=command.tenant_id,\n                user_id=command.user_id,\n            )\n            return {\"status\": \"ok\", \"hp\": command.new_hp}\n\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    #                         Quick manual smoke-test                       #\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n\n    ecs = InMemoryECS()\n    bus = CommandBus(ecs_gateway=ecs)\n\n    result = bus.dispatch(\n        SetHealth(\n            entity_id=\"player-123\",\n            tenant_id=\"acme-corp\",\n            user_id=\"gm-1\",\n            new_hp=95,\n        )\n    )\n    print(\"RESULT:\", result)\n```", "numLines": 446, "startLine": 1, "totalLines": 446}}

1→```python
     2→"""
     3→ledgerquest.services.auditing.log_writer
     4→---------------------------------------
     5→
     6→A thin yet robust audit trail writer that fulfils the multi-tenant compliance
     7→requirements of LedgerQuest Engine.  The writer batches audit log records and
     8→persists them to a DynamoDB table while also emitting them to the Python
     9→logging subsystem for CloudWatch log shipping.
    10→
    11→Design goals
    12→============
    13→
    14→1.  **Low-latency hot path** – game logic must not block on I/O.
    15→2.  **Serverless constraints** – executions may be frozen or force-ended at any
    16→    time.  Provide an explicit `flush()` so Lambda handlers can persist
    17→    buffered events right before returning.
    18→3.  **Cost-optimised** – leverage `batch_write_item` (25 items) to minimise API
    19→    calls.
    20→4.  **Defensive** – retries with decorrelated jitter and partial-failure
    21→    detection.
    22→"""
    23→
    24→from __future__ import annotations
    25→
    26→import json
    27→import logging
    28→import os
    29→import random
    30→import threading
    31→import time
    32→from dataclasses import asdict, dataclass, field
    33→from datetime import datetime, timezone
    34→from itertools import islice
    35→from typing import Any, Dict, Iterable, List, Optional
    36→
    37→import boto3
    38→from botocore.exceptions import BotoCoreError, ClientError
    39→
    40→__all__ = ["AuditLogRecord", "LogWriter"]
    41→
    42→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    43→# Configuration
    44→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    45→
    46→_DEFAULT_TABLE_NAME = os.getenv("LQ_ENGINE_AUDIT_TABLE", "ledgerquest-audit")
    47→_MAX_BATCH_SIZE = 25  # DynamoDB limit
    48→_MAX_RETRIES = 5
    49→_BASE_BACKOFF = 0.1  # seconds
    50→_LOGGER = logging.getLogger("ledgerquest.audit")
    51→
    52→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    53→# Data Model
    54→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    55→
    56→
    57→@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
    58→class AuditLogRecord:
    59→    """
    60→    A single immutable audit-trail entry.
    61→
    62→    NOTE:
    63→        Keep the footprint small – this struct may be instantiated thousands of
    64→        times per second during peak multiplayer sessions.
    65→    """
    66→
    67→    tenant_id: str
    68→    user_id: str
    69→    session_id: str
    70→    action: str
    71→    entity_type: str
    72→    entity_id: str
    73→    metadata: Dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
    74→    timestamp: datetime = field(
    75→        default_factory=lambda: datetime.now(tz=timezone.utc)
    76→    )
    77→
    78→    def to_ddb_item(self) -> Dict[str, Dict[str, Any]]:
    79→        """
    80→        Convert this record into the DynamoDB attribute-value map format.
    81→
    82→        Partition / sort key schema:
    83→            PK = TENANT#<tenant_id>
    84→            SK = <ts_iso>#<session_id>#<action>
    85→
    86→        Secondary index on `entity_id` enables look-ups by domain object.
    87→        """
    88→        iso_ts = self.timestamp.isoformat()
    89→
    90→        return {
    91→            "PK": {"S": f"TENANT#{self.tenant_id}"},
    92→            "SK": {"S": f"{iso_ts}#{self.session_id}#{self.action}"},
    93→            "entity_type": {"S": self.entity_type},
    94→            "entity_id": {"S": self.entity_id},
    95→            "user_id": {"S": self.user_id},
    96→            "session_id": {"S": self.session_id},
    97→            "action": {"S": self.action},
    98→            "timestamp": {"S": iso_ts},
    99→            # Serialise metadata as JSON string.  DDB 'M' types must have
   100→            # homogeneous value types, which cannot be guaranteed here.
   101→            "metadata": {"S": json.dumps(self.metadata, default=_json_fallback)},
   102→        }
   103→
   104→    def to_json(self) -> str:
   105→        """Human-readable JSON representation used in CloudWatch logs."""
   106→        payload = asdict(self)
   107→        payload["timestamp"] = self.timestamp.isoformat()
   108→        return json.dumps(payload, default=_json_fallback)
   109→
   110→
   111→def _json_fallback(o: Any) -> str:
   112→    """Best-effort JSON serialiser for otherwise unsupported objects."""
   113→    try:
   114→        return str(o)
   115→    except Exception:  # pragma: no cover
   116→        return "<unserialisable>"
   117→
   118→
   119→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   120→# Helper utilities
   121→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   122→
   123→
   124→def _chunks(iterable: Iterable[Any], size: int) -> Iterable[List[Any]]:
   125→    """Yield successive *size* chunks from *iterable*."""
   126→    iterator = iter(iterable)
   127→    while chunk := list(islice(iterator, size)):
   128→        yield chunk
   129→
   130→
   131→def _sleep_with_jitter(attempt: int) -> None:
   132→    """
   133→    Exponential back-off with full jitter, as per AWS architecture guidelines.
   134→
   135→    delay = random_between(0, base * 2**attempt)
   136→    """
   137→    delay = random.uniform(0, _BASE_BACKOFF * (2**attempt))
   138→    time.sleep(delay)
   139→
   140→
   141→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   142→# LogWriter
   143→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   144→
   145→
   146→class LogWriter:
   147→    """
   148→    Thread-safe, batching audit log writer.
   149→
   150→    Basic usage inside a Lambda handler::
   151→
   152→        log_writer = LogWriter()
   153→        # ... game loop / business logic ...
   154→        log_writer.write(
   155→            tenant_id=ctx.tenant,
   156→            user_id=ctx.user,
   157→            session_id=ctx.session,
   158→            action="PLAYER_MOVED",
   159→            entity_type="avatar",
   160→            entity_id=player_id,
   161→            metadata={"x": 42, "y": 21},
   162→        )
   163→        # ensure we persisted all buffered events
   164→        log_writer.flush()
   165→    """
   166→
   167→    _lock: threading.Lock
   168→    _buffer: List[AuditLogRecord]
   169→    _client: "boto3.client"
   170→
   171→    def __init__(
   172→        self,
   173→        dynamodb_table: str | None = None,
   174→        aws_region: str | None = None,
   175→        boto3_client: Optional["boto3.client"] = None,
   176→    ) -> None:
   177→        self._table_name = dynamodb_table or _DEFAULT_TABLE_NAME
   178→        self._client = boto3_client or boto3.client(
   179→            "dynamodb", region_name=aws_region
   180→        )
   181→        self._buffer = []
   182→        self._lock = threading.Lock()
   183→
   184→    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ Public API
   185→
   186→    def write(
   187→        self,
   188→        *,
   189→        tenant_id: str,
   190→        user_id: str,
   191→        session_id: str,
   192→        action: str,
   193→        entity_type: str,
   194→        entity_id: str,
   195→        metadata: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,
   196→        timestamp: Optional[datetime] = None,
   197→    ) -> None:
   198→        """
   199→        Buffer an audit event.
   200→
   201→        Non-blocking; performs minimal work and returns immediately.
   202→        """
   203→        record = AuditLogRecord(
   204→            tenant_id=tenant_id,
   205→            user_id=user_id,
   206→            session_id=session_id,
   207→            action=action,
   208→            entity_type=entity_type,
   209→            entity_id=entity_id,
   210→            metadata=metadata or {},
   211→            timestamp=timestamp or datetime.now(tz=timezone.utc),
   212→        )
   213→
   214→        with self._lock:
   215→            self._buffer.append(record)
   216→            buffer_len = len(self._buffer)
   217→
   218→        _LOGGER.debug(
   219→            "Buffered audit record (%s). Current buffer size=%d",
   220→            record.action,
   221→            buffer_len,
   222→        )
   223→
   224→        if buffer_len >= _MAX_BATCH_SIZE:
   225→            self.flush()
   226→
   227→    def flush(self) -> None:
   228→        """
   229→        Write all buffered records to DynamoDB.
   230→
   231→        Retries transient errors automatically.  On unrecoverable failure the
   232→        records are *not* dropped – they remain in the buffer so that the caller
   233→        can retry or persist them elsewhere (e.g. S3 DLQ).
   234→        """
   235→        with self._lock:
   236→            if not self._buffer:
   237→                _LOGGER.debug("No audit records to flush.")
   238→                return
   239→
   240→            records = self._buffer
   241→            self._buffer = []
   242→
   243→        _LOGGER.debug("Flushing %d audit records ...", len(records))
   244→
   245→        for chunk in _chunks(records, _MAX_BATCH_SIZE):
   246→            # Translate to DynamoDB format once to avoid repeated costs in retry loop
   247→            request_items = [
   248→                {"PutRequest": {"Item": rec.to_ddb_item()}} for rec in chunk
   249→            ]
   250→            self._batch_put_with_retry(request_items)
   251→
   252→            # Even if the write fails, we still log the record in CloudWatch
   253→            for rec in chunk:
   254→                _LOGGER.info(rec.to_json())
   255→
   256→    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ Internals
   257→
   258→    def _batch_put_with_retry(self, items: List[Dict[str, Any]]) -> None:
   259→        """
   260→        `batch_write_item` with partial unprocessed handling and retries.
   261→
   262→        Adopt the pattern recommended in
   263→        https://docs.aws.amazon.com/amazondynamodb/latest/developerguide/example-python-write-batch.html
   264→        """
   265→        attempt = 0
   266→        unprocessed = items
   267→
   268→        while unprocessed and attempt < _MAX_RETRIES:
   269→            try:
   270→                response = self._client.batch_write_item(
   271→                    RequestItems={self._table_name: unprocessed},
   272→                    ReturnConsumedCapacity="NONE",
   273→                )
   274→                unprocessed = response.get("UnprocessedItems", {}).get(
   275→                    self._table_name, []
   276→                )
   277→            except (ClientError, BotoCoreError) as exc:
   278→                _LOGGER.warning(
   279→                    "DynamoDB batch_write_item failed on attempt %d/%d: %s",
   280→                    attempt + 1,
   281→                    _MAX_RETRIES,
   282→                    exc,
   283→                    exc_info=_LOGGER.isEnabledFor(logging.DEBUG),
   284→                )
   285→                _sleep_with_jitter(attempt)
   286→            else:
   287→                if unprocessed:
   288→                    _LOGGER.debug(
   289→                        "%d items unprocessed, retrying ...", len(unprocessed)
   290→                    )
   291→                    _sleep_with_jitter(attempt)
   292→            attempt += 1
   293→
   294→        if unprocessed:
   295→            # Re-enqueue unprocessed items back to buffer for caller to decide.
   296→            with self._lock:
   297→                self._buffer.extend(
   298→                    AuditLogRecord(
   299→                        tenant_id=item["PutRequest"]["Item"]["PK"]["S"].split(
   300→                            "#", maxsplit=1
   301→                        )[1],
   302→                        user_id=item["PutRequest"]["Item"]["user_id"]["S"],
   303→                        session_id=item["PutRequest"]["Item"]["session_id"]["S"],
   304→                        action=item["PutRequest"]["Item"]["action"]["S"],
   305→                        entity_type=item["PutRequest"]["Item"]["entity_type"][
   306→                            "S"
   307→                        ],
   308→                        entity_id=item["PutRequest"]["Item"]["entity_id"]["S"],
   309→                        metadata=json.loads(
   310→                            item["PutRequest"]["Item"]["metadata"]["S"]
   311→                        ),
   312→                        timestamp=datetime.fromisoformat(
   313→                            item["PutRequest"]["Item"]["timestamp"]["S"]
   314→                        ),
   315→                    )
   316→                    for item in unprocessed
   317→                )
   318→            raise RuntimeError(
   319→                f"Failed to write {len(unprocessed)} audit records after "
   320→                f"{_MAX_RETRIES} attempts; re-queued for later flush."
   321→            )
   322→```
