ensure we persisted all buffered events\n        log_writer.flush()\n    \"\"\"\n\n    _lock: threading.Lock\n    _buffer: List[AuditLogRecord]\n    _client: \"boto3.client\"\n\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        dynamodb_table: str | None = None,\n        aws_region: str | None = None,\n        boto3_client: Optional[\"boto3.client\"] = None,\n    ) -> None:\n        self._table_name = dynamodb_table or _DEFAULT_TABLE_NAME\n        self._client = boto3_client or boto3.client(\n            \"dynamodb\", region_name=aws_region\n        )\n        self._buffer = []\n        self._lock = threading.Lock()\n\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ Public API\n\n    def write(\n        self,\n        *,\n        tenant_id: str,\n        user_id: str,\n        session_id: str,\n        action: str,\n        entity_type: str,\n        entity_id: str,\n        metadata: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,\n        timestamp: Optional[datetime] = None,\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Buffer an audit event.\n\n        Non-blocking; performs minimal work and returns immediately.\n        \"\"\"\n        record = AuditLogRecord(\n            tenant_id=tenant_id,\n            user_id=user_id,\n            session_id=session_id,\n            action=action,\n            entity_type=entity_type,\n            entity_id=entity_id,\n            metadata=metadata or {},\n            timestamp=timestamp or datetime.now(tz=timezone.utc),\n        )\n\n        with self._lock:\n            self._buffer.append(record)\n            buffer_len = len(self._buffer)\n\n        _LOGGER.debug(\n            \"Buffered audit record (%s). Current buffer size=%d\",\n            record.action,\n            buffer_len,\n        )\n\n        if buffer_len >= _MAX_BATCH_SIZE:\n            self.flush()\n\n    def flush(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Write all buffered records to DynamoDB.\n\n        Retries transient errors automatically.  On unrecoverable failure the\n        records are *not* dropped – they remain in the buffer so that the caller\n        can retry or persist them elsewhere (e.g. S3 DLQ).\n        \"\"\"\n        with self._lock:\n            if not self._buffer:\n                _LOGGER.debug(\"No audit records to flush.\")\n                return\n\n            records = self._buffer\n            self._buffer = []\n\n        _LOGGER.debug(\"Flushing %d audit records ...\", len(records))\n\n        for chunk in _chunks(records, _MAX_BATCH_SIZE):\n            # Translate to DynamoDB format once to avoid repeated costs in retry loop\n            request_items = [\n                {\"PutRequest\": {\"Item\": rec.to_ddb_item()}} for rec in chunk\n            ]\n            self._batch_put_with_retry(request_items)\n\n            # Even if the write fails, we still log the record in CloudWatch\n            for rec in chunk:\n                _LOGGER.info(rec.to_json())\n\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ Internals\n\n    def _batch_put_with_retry(self, items: List[Dict[str, Any]]) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        `batch_write_item` with partial unprocessed handling and retries.\n\n        Adopt the pattern recommended in\n        https://docs.aws.amazon.com/amazondynamodb/latest/developerguide/example-python-write-batch.html\n        \"\"\"\n        attempt = 0\n        unprocessed = items\n\n        while unprocessed and attempt < _MAX_RETRIES:\n            try:\n                response = self._client.batch_write_item(\n                    RequestItems={self._table_name: unprocessed},\n                    ReturnConsumedCapacity=\"NONE\",\n                )\n                unprocessed = response.get(\"UnprocessedItems\", {}).get(\n                    self._table_name, []\n                )\n            except (ClientError, BotoCoreError) as exc:\n                _LOGGER.warning(\n                    \"DynamoDB batch_write_item failed on attempt %d/%d: %s\",\n                    attempt + 1,\n                    _MAX_RETRIES,\n                    exc,\n                    exc_info=_LOGGER.isEnabledFor(logging.DEBUG),\n                )\n                _sleep_with_jitter(attempt)\n            else:\n                if unprocessed:\n                    _LOGGER.debug(\n                        \"%d items unprocessed, retrying ...\", len(unprocessed)\n                    )\n                    _sleep_with_jitter(attempt)\n            attempt += 1\n\n        if unprocessed:\n            # Re-enqueue unprocessed items back to buffer for caller to decide.\n            with self._lock:\n                self._buffer.extend(\n                    AuditLogRecord(\n                        tenant_id=item[\"PutRequest\"][\"Item\"][\"PK\"][\"S\"].split(\n                            \"#\", maxsplit=1\n                        )[1],\n                        user_id=item[\"PutRequest\"][\"Item\"][\"user_id\"][\"S\"],\n                        session_id=item[\"PutRequest\"][\"Item\"][\"session_id\"][\"S\"],\n                        action=item[\"PutRequest\"][\"Item\"][\"action\"][\"S\"],\n                        entity_type=item[\"PutRequest\"][\"Item\"][\"entity_type\"][\n                            \"S\"\n                        ],\n                        entity_id=item[\"PutRequest\"][\"Item\"][\"entity_id\"][\"S\"],\n                        metadata=json.loads(\n                            item[\"PutRequest\"][\"Item\"][\"metadata\"][\"S\"]\n                        ),\n                        timestamp=datetime.fromisoformat(\n                            item[\"PutRequest\"][\"Item\"][\"timestamp\"][\"S\"]\n                        ),\n                    )\n                    for item in unprocessed\n                )\n            raise RuntimeError(\n                f\"Failed to write {len(unprocessed)} audit records after \"\n                f\"{_MAX_RETRIES} attempts; re-queued for later flush.\"\n            )\n```", "numLines": 322, "startLine": 1, "totalLines": 322}}

1→```python
     2→"""
     3→LedgerQuest Engine – Physics Simulator
     4→======================================
     5→
     6→This module contains a stateless physics simulator that is intended to be
     7→invoked as an AWS Lambda (or any FaaS runtime).  The simulator receives a
     8→serialized “world-slice” describing entities that contain physics components,
     9→advances the simulation by `delta_time`, and returns the new state.
    10→
    11→Unlike traditional long-running simulations, LedgerQuest keeps no in-memory
    12→state between invocations—making the update routine *purely functional* and
    13→therefore horizontally scalable.  Long-lived state is externalised to
    14→DynamoDB/S3 by higher-level orchestration layers.
    15→
    16→Key Features
    17→------------
    18→• 2-D rigid-body integration with semi-implicit Euler
    19→• Broad-phase AABB collision culling
    20→• Narrow-phase contact resolution (elastic impulse)
    21→• Deterministic float math w/ configurable tolerance
    22→• Structured logging for audit/trace pipelines
    23→• Graceful error handling with typed exceptions
    24→
    25→Notes
    26→-----
    27→This implementation purposely avoids heavyweight, C-accelerated libraries
    28→(e.g. PyBox2D, Pymunk) so that it can run inside Lambda’s execution limits
    29→without native extensions.  If you deploy this in a container with those
    30→libraries available, simply replace the math backend while keeping the
    31→function signatures stable.
    32→"""
    33→from __future__ import annotations
    34→
    35→import json
    36→import logging
    37→import math
    38→import os
    39→import uuid
    40→from dataclasses import dataclass, field, asdict
    41→from typing import Dict, Iterable, List, Optional, Tuple
    42→
    43→# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
    44→# Logging Configuration
    45→# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
    46→
    47→LOG_LEVEL = os.getenv("LEDGERQUEST_PHYSICS_LOG_LEVEL", "INFO").upper()
    48→logging.basicConfig(
    49→    level=LOG_LEVEL,
    50→    format="%(asctime)s | %(levelname)s | %(name)s | %(message)s",
    51→)
    52→logger = logging.getLogger("ledgerquest.engine.physics.simulator")
    53→
    54→
    55→# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
    56→# Exceptions
    57→# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
    58→
    59→class PhysicsError(RuntimeError):
    60→    """Base-class for physics-simulation errors."""
    61→
    62→
    63→class DeserializationError(PhysicsError):
    64→    """Invalid or malformed input payload raised during deserialization."""
    65→
    66→
    67→class SimulationInvariantViolation(PhysicsError):
    68→    """Raised when the simulation produces a non-finite or invalid result."""
    69→
    70→
    71→# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
    72→# Math Primitives
    73→# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
    74→
    75→@dataclass(slots=True, frozen=True)
    76→class Vec2:
    77→    """Immutable 2-D vector with basic operations—fast enough for small N."""
    78→    x: float
    79→    y: float
    80→
    81→    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    82→    # Vector arithmetic
    83→    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    84→    def __add__(self, other: "Vec2") -> "Vec2":
    85→        return Vec2(self.x + other.x, self.y + other.y)
    86→
    87→    def __sub__(self, other: "Vec2") -> "Vec2":
    88→        return Vec2(self.x - other.x, self.y - other.y)
    89→
    90→    def __mul__(self, scalar: float) -> "Vec2":
    91→        return Vec2(self.x * scalar, self.y * scalar)
    92→
    93→    __rmul__ = __mul__
    94→
    95→    def dot(self, other: "Vec2") -> float:
    96→        return (self.x * other.x) + (self.y * other.y)
    97→
    98→    def magnitude(self) -> float:
    99→        return math.hypot(self.x, self.y)
   100→
   101→    def normalize(self) -> "Vec2":
   102→        mag = self.magnitude() or 1.0
   103→        return Vec2(self.x / mag, self.y / mag)
   104→
   105→    def perpendicular(self) -> "Vec2":
   106→        """Right-handed perpendicular vector (rotated 90°)."""
   107→        return Vec2(-self.y, self.x)
   108→
   109→    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
   110→    # Built-ins helpers
   111→    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
   112→    def __iter__(self):
   113→        yield self.x
   114→        yield self.y
   115→
   116→    def to_json(self) -> Tuple[float, float]:
   117→        return self.x, self.y
   118→
   119→    @staticmethod
   120→    def from_iterable(it: Iterable[float]) -> "Vec2":
   121→        x, y, *_ = it
   122→        return Vec2(float(x), float(y))
   123→
   124→
   125→# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   126→# Physics Data Structures
   127→# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   128→
   129→@dataclass(slots=True)
   130→class AABB:
   131→    """Axis-Aligned Bounding Box used for broad-phase collision tests."""
   132→    min: Vec2
   133→    max: Vec2
   134→
   135→    def overlaps(self, other: "AABB") -> bool:
   136→        return (
   137→            self.max.x >= other.min.x
   138→            and self.min.x <= other.max.x
   139→            and self.max.y >= other.min.y
   140→            and self.min.y <= other.max.y
   141→        )
   142→
   143→    @classmethod
   144→    def from_center(cls, center: Vec2, half_extents: Vec2) -> "AABB":
   145→        return cls(
   146→            min=center - half_extents,
   147→            max=center + half_extents,
   148→        )
   149→
   150→
   151→@dataclass(slots=True)
   152→class RigidBody:
   153→    """
   154→    Minimal rigid-body representation.
   155→
   156→    Only rectangle shapes are supported in this reference implementation, but
   157→    the component data model can be extended without changing the simulator’s
   158→    public interface.
   159→    """
   160→    id: str
   161→    position: Vec2
   162→    velocity: Vec2
   163→    half_extents: Vec2  # half-width/height (meters)
   164→    mass: float = 1.0
   165→    restitution: float = 0.5
   166→    static: bool = False
   167→    # Non-serialized runtime fields
   168→    force_acc: Vec2 = field(default_factory=lambda: Vec2(0.0, 0.0), repr=False)
   169→
   170→    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
   171→    # Convenience wrappers
   172→    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
   173→    @property
   174→    def inv_mass(self) -> float:
   175→        """Inverse mass—0 for static or infinite-mass bodies."""
   176→        return 0.0 if self.static else 1.0 / self.mass
   177→
   178→    @property
   179→    def aabb(self) -> AABB:
   180→        return AABB.from_center(self.position, self.half_extents)
   181→
   182→    def apply_force(self, force: Vec2):
   183→        """Accumulate force for the current frame (F = m * a)."""
   184→        if not self.static:
   185→            self.force_acc = self.force_acc + force
   186→
   187→
   188→# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   189→# Physics Simulator
   190→# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   191→
   192→class PhysicsSimulator:
   193→    """
   194→    Stateless 2-D physics simulator.
   195→
   196→    Usage (inside Lambda):
   197→    ----------------------
   198→    def handler(event, context):
   199→        return PhysicsSimulator.lambda_handler(event, context)
   200→    """
   201→
   202→    # Gravity can be overridden at runtime via env-var for gameplay tuning.
   203→    GRAVITY = Vec2(
   204→        float(os.getenv("LEDGERQUEST_GRAVITY_X", "0")),
   205→        float(os.getenv("LEDGERQUEST_GRAVITY_Y", "-9.81")),
   206→    )
   207→
   208→    # Velocity threshold below which bodies will be considered “sleeping”.
   209→    SLEEP_TOLERANCE = float(os.getenv("LEDGERQUEST_SLEEP_TOLERANCE", "0.01"))
   210→
   211→    # Safe-guard to avoid unbounded compute for huge payloads.
   212→    MAX_BODIES_PER_INVOCATION = int(os.getenv("LEDGERQUEST_MAX_BODIES", "512"))
   213→
   214→    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
   215→    # Lambda Entrypoint
   216→    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
   217→    @classmethod
   218→    def lambda_handler(cls, event, _context):
   219→        """
   220→        AWS Lambda handler. Receives JSON payload:
   221→        {
   222→          "delta_time": 0.016,
   223→          "bodies": [
   224→              {
   225→                  "id": "entity-uuid",
   226→                  "position": [x, y],
   227→                  "velocity": [vx, vy],
   228→                  "half_extents": [hx, hy],
   229→                  "mass": 1.0,
   230→                  "restitution": 0.5,
   231→                  "static": false
   232→              },
   233→              ...
   234→          ]
   235→        }
   236→        """
   237→        try:
   238→            world_state = cls._deserialize_event(event)
   239→        except DeserializationError as exc:
   240→            logger.exception("Invalid input payload.")
   241→            raise
   242→
   243→        logger.debug("Simulating %d bodies (dt=%.6f)",
   244→                     len(world_state), event["delta_time"])
   245→
   246→        cls._validate_payload_size(len(world_state))
   247→
   248→        cls._integrate(world_state, event["delta_time"])
   249→        cls._broad_and_narrow_phase(world_state)
   250→
   251→        # Prepare JSON-serialisable response
   252→        response = {
   253→            "bodies": [
   254→                {
   255→                    "id": body.id,
   256→                    "position": body.position.to_json(),
   257→                    "velocity": body.velocity.to_json(),
   258→                }
   259→                for body in world_state
   260→            ]
   261→        }
   262→        return json.dumps(response)
   263→
   264→    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
   265→    # Public helper for unit tests or in-process calls
   266→    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
   267→    @classmethod
   268→    def step(
   269→        cls,
   270→        bodies: List[RigidBody],
   271→        delta_time: float,
   272→        *,
   273→        apply_gravity: bool = True,
   274→    ) -> List[RigidBody]:
   275→        """
   276→        Advance simulation by `delta_time` and return *mutated* bodies list.
   277→
   278→        The method is kept pure: no network, no I/O, no global state mutation,
   279→        which makes it deterministic and easy to unit-test.
   280→        """
   281→        if apply_gravity:
   282→            for body in bodies:
   283→                if not body.static:
   284→                    body.apply_force(cls.GRAVITY * body.mass)
   285→
   286→        cls._integrate(bodies, delta_time)
   287→        cls._broad_and_narrow_phase(bodies)
   288→        return bodies
   289→
   290→    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
   291→    # Internal – Payload (de)serialization
   292→    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
   293→    @staticmethod
   294→    def _deserialize_event(event: Dict) -> List[RigidBody]:
   295→        """
   296→        Convert the inbound JSON event into a list of `RigidBody` instances.
   297→        Raises `DeserializationError` when invalid.
   298→        """
   299→        try:
   300→            bodies_payload = event["bodies"]
   301→            delta_time = float(event["delta_time"])
   302→            if delta_time <= 0.0 or not math.isfinite(delta_time):
   303→                raise ValueError("delta_time must be positive and finite.")
   304→        except (KeyError, TypeError, ValueError) as exc:
   305→            raise DeserializationError(str(exc)) from exc
   306→
   307→        bodies = []
   308→        for raw in bodies_payload:
   309→            try:
   310→                body = RigidBody(
   311→                    id=raw.get("id") or str(uuid.uuid4()),
   312→                    position=Vec2.from_iterable(raw["position"]),
   313→                    velocity=Vec2.from_iterable(raw["velocity"]),
   314→                    half_extents=Vec2.from_iterable(raw["half_extents"]),
   315→                    mass=float(raw.get("mass", 1.0)),
   316→                    restitution=float(raw.get("restitution", 0.5)),
   317→                    static=bool(raw.get("static", False)),
   318→                )
   319→                bodies.append(body)
   320→            except Exception as exc:
   321→                raise DeserializationError(
   322→                    f"Malformed body entry: {raw}"
   323→                ) from exc
   324→
   325→        return bodies
   326→
   327→    @classmethod
   328→    def _validate_payload_size(cls, n: int):
   329→        if n > cls.MAX_BODIES_PER_INVOCATION:
   330→            raise PhysicsError(
   331→                f"Too many bodies for a single invocation "
   332→                f"({n} > {cls.MAX_BODIES_PER_INVOCATION})."
   333→            )
   334→
   335→    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
   336→    # Internal – Integration
   337→    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
   338→    @staticmethod
   339→    def _integrate(bodies: Iterable[RigidBody], dt: float):
   340→        """Semi-implicit Euler integration."""
   341→        for body in bodies:
   342→            if body.static:
   343→                continue
   344→
   345→            # a = F * inv_mass
   346→            acceleration = body.force_acc * body.inv_mass
   347→            body.velocity = body.velocity + acceleration * dt
   348→            body.position = body.position + body.velocity * dt
   349→
   350→            # Clear forces for next frame
   351→            body.force_acc = Vec2(0.0, 0.0)
   352→
   353→            # Sleep optimisation
   354→            if body.velocity.magnitude() < PhysicsSimulator.SLEEP_TOLERANCE:
   355→                body.velocity = Vec2(0.0, 0.0)
   356→
   357→            # Post-integration invariant check
   358→            if (
   359→                not math.isfinite(body.position.x)
   360→                or not math.isfinite(body.position.y)
   361→            ):
   362→                raise SimulationInvariantViolation(
   363→                    f"Non-finite position for body {body.id}"
   364→                )
   365→
   366→    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
   367→    # Internal – Collision Phases
   368→    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
   369→    @staticmethod
   370→    def _broad_and_narrow_phase(bodies: List[RigidBody]):
   371→        """
   372→        Perform broad-phase AABB checks followed by narrow-phase impulse
   373→        resolution for overlapping pairs.
   374→        """
   375→        # Simple N^2 broadphase due to low `MAX_BODIES_PER_INVOCATION`.
   376→        for i, a in enumerate(bodies):
   377→            if a.static:
   378→                continue
   379→            for b in bodies[i + 1 :]:
   380→                # Skip pairs with two static bodies
   381→                if a.static and b.static:
   382→                    continue
   383→
   384→                if not a.aabb.overlaps(b.aabb):
   385→                    continue  # broad-phase cull
   386→
   387→                PhysicsSimulator._resolve_collision(a, b)
   388→
   389→    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
   390→    # Internal – Narrow-phase Resolution
   391→    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
   392→    @staticmethod
   393→    def _resolve_collision(a: RigidBody, b: RigidBody):
   394→        """
   395→        Very small subset of SAT—for axis-aligned rectangles only.
   396→        Calculates penetration depth along each axis and resolves along the
   397→        axis of least penetration.
   398→        """
   399→        # Relative vector
   400→        n = b.position - a.position
   401→
   402→        # Overlap on x axis
   403→        x_overlap = (a.half_extents.x + b.half_extents.x) - abs(n.x)
   404→        if x_overlap <= 0:
   405→            return  # no collision
   406→
   407→        # Overlap on y axis
   408→        y_overlap = (a.half_extents.y + b.half_extents.y) - abs(n.y)
   409→        if y_overlap <= 0:
   410→            return  # no collision
   411→
   412→        # Find axis of least penetration
   413→        if x_overlap < y_overlap:
   414→            mtv = Vec2(x_overlap if n.x > 0 else -x_overlap, 0)
   415→        else:
   416→            mtv = Vec2(0, y_overlap if n.y > 0 else -y_overlap)
   417→
   418→        # Positional correction (minimum translation vector)
   419→        PhysicsSimulator._positional_correction(a, b, mtv)
   420→
   421→        # Relative velocity
   422→        rv = b.velocity - a.velocity
   423→        # Normal is direction of MTV
   424→        normal = mtv.normalize()
   425→
   426→        vel_along_normal = rv.dot(normal)
   427→        if vel_along_normal > 0:
   428→            return  # bodies separating
   429→
   430→        restitution = min(a.restitution, b.restitution)
   431→
   432→        # Impulse scalar
   433→        j = -(1 + restitution) * vel_along_normal
   434→        inv_mass_sum = a.inv_mass + b.inv_mass
   435→        if inv_mass_sum == 0:
   436→            return
   437→
   438→        j /= inv_mass_sum
   439→
   440→        # Apply impulse
   441→        impulse = normal * j
   442→        if not a.static:
   443→            a.velocity = a.velocity - impulse * a.inv_mass
   444→        if not b.static:
   445→            b.velocity = b.velocity + impulse * b.inv_mass
   446→
   447→    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
   448→    # Internal – Positional Correction
   449→    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
   450→    @staticmethod
   451→    def _positional_correction(a: RigidBody, b: RigidBody, mtv: Vec2):
   452→        """
   453→        Positional correction to avoid sinking due to numerical errors.
   454→        """
   455→        percent = 0.8  # higher percentage = more correction
   456→        slop = 0.01    # penetration allowance
   457→        correction_magnitude = max(mtv.magnitude() - slop, 0.0) * percent
   458→        correction = mtv.normalize() * correction_magnitude
   459→
   460→        total_inv_mass = a.inv_mass + b.inv_mass
   461→        if total_inv_mass == 0:
   462→            return
   463→
   464→        if not a.static:
   465→            a.position = a.position - correction * (a.inv_mass / total_inv_mass)
   466→        if not b.static:
   467→            b.position = b.position + correction * (b.inv_mass / total_inv_mass)
   468→
   469→
   470→# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   471→# Module test hook (executed only when run locally)
   472→# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   473→if __name__ == "__main__":
   474→    # Quick sanity test – two boxes falling onto the floor.
   475→    ground = RigidBody(
   476→        id="ground",
   477→        position=Vec2(0.0, -1.0),
   478→        velocity=Vec2(0.0, 0.0),
   479→        half_extents=Vec2(5.0, 0.5),
   480→        static=True,
   481→    )
   482→
   483→    box = RigidBody(
   484→        id="box",
   485→        position=Vec2(0.0, 2.0),
   486→        velocity=Vec2(0.0, 0.0),
   487→        half_extents=Vec2(0.5, 0.5),
   488→        mass=1.0,
   489→        restitution=0.3,
   490→    )
   491→
   492→    bodies = [ground, box]
   493→    dt = 1.0 / 60.0
   494→
   495→    for frame in range(240):  # 4 seconds
   496→        PhysicsSimulator.step(bodies, dt)
   497→        logger.info(
   498→            "Frame %03d | pos=%.3f, %.3f vel=%.3f, %.3f",
   499→            frame,
   500→            box.position.x,
   501→            box.position.y,
   502→            box.velocity.x,
   503→            box.velocity.y,
   504→        )
   505→```

[metadata] {"type": "text", "file": {"filePath": "/app/project/ledgerquest/engine/physics/simulator.py", "content": "```python\n\"\"\"\nLedgerQuest Engine – Physics Simulator\n======================================\n\nThis module contains a stateless physics simulator that is intended to be\ninvoked as an AWS Lambda (or any FaaS runtime).  The simulator receives a\nserialized “world-slice” describing entities that contain physics components,\nadvances the simulation by `delta_time`, and returns the new state.\n\nUnlike traditional long-running simulations, LedgerQuest keeps no in-memory\nstate between invocations—making the update routine *purely functional* and\ntherefore horizontally scalable.  Long-lived state is externalised to\nDynamoDB/S3 by higher-level orchestration layers.\n\nKey Features\n------------\n• 2-D rigid-body integration with semi-implicit Euler\n• Broad-phase AABB collision culling\n• Narrow-phase contact resolution (elastic impulse)\n• Deterministic float math w/ configurable tolerance\n• Structured logging for audit/trace pipelines\n• Graceful error handling with typed exceptions\n\nNotes\n-----\nThis implementation purposely avoids heavyweight, C-accelerated libraries\n(e.g. PyBox2D, Pymunk) so that it can run inside Lambda’s execution limits\nwithout native extensions.  If you deploy this in a container with those\nlibraries available, simply replace the math backend while keeping the\nfunction signatures stable.\n\"\"\"\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport json\nimport logging\nimport math\nimport os\nimport uuid\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass, field, asdict\nfrom typing import Dict, Iterable, List, Optional, Tuple\n\n# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Logging Configuration\n# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nLOG_LEVEL = os.getenv(\"LEDGERQUEST_PHYSICS_LOG_LEVEL\", \"INFO\").upper()\nlogging.basicConfig(\n    level=LOG_LEVEL,\n    format=\"%(asctime)s | %(levelname)s | %(name)s | %(message)s\",\n)\nlogger = logging.getLogger(\"ledgerquest.engine.physics.simulator\")\n\n\n# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Exceptions\n# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nclass PhysicsError(RuntimeError):\n    \"\"\"Base-class for physics-simulation errors.\"\"\"\n\n\nclass DeserializationError(PhysicsError):\n    \"\"\"Invalid or malformed input payload raised during deserialization.\"\"\"\n\n\nclass SimulationInvariantViolation(PhysicsError):\n    \"\"\"Raised when the simulation produces a non-finite or invalid result.\"\"\"\n\n\n# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Math Primitives\n# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n@dataclass(slots=True, frozen=True)\nclass Vec2:\n    \"\"\"Immutable 2-D vector with basic operations—fast enough for small N.\"\"\"\n    x: float\n    y: float\n\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n    # Vector arithmetic\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n    def __add__(self, other: \"Vec2\") -> \"Vec2\":\n        return Vec2(self.x + other.x, self.y + other.y)\n\n    def __sub__(self, other: \"Vec2\") -> \"Vec2\":\n        return Vec2(self.x - other.x, self.y - other.y)\n\n    def __mul__(self, scalar: float) -> \"Vec2\":\n        return Vec2(self.x * scalar, self.y * scalar)\n\n    __rmul__ = __mul__\n\n    def dot(self, other: \"Vec2\") -> float:\n        return (self.x * other.x) + (self.y * other.y)\n\n    def magnitude(self) -> float:\n        return math.hypot(self.x, self.y)\n\n    def normalize(self) -> \"Vec2\":\n        mag = self.magnitude() or 1.0\n        return Vec2(self.x / mag, self.y / mag)\n\n    def perpendicular(self) -> \"Vec2\":\n        \"\"\"Right-handed perpendicular vector (rotated 90°).\"\"\"\n        return Vec2(-self.y, self.x)\n\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n    # Built-ins helpers\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n    def __iter__(self):\n        yield self.x\n        yield self.y\n\n    def to_json(self) -> Tuple[float, float]:\n        return self.x, self.y\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def from_iterable(it: Iterable[float]) -> \"Vec2\":\n        x, y, *_ = it\n        return Vec2(float(x), float(y))\n\n\n# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Physics Data Structures\n# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n@dataclass(slots=True)\nclass AABB:\n    \"\"\"Axis-Aligned Bounding Box used for broad-phase collision tests.\"\"\"\n    min: Vec2\n    max: Vec2\n\n    def overlaps(self, other: \"AABB\") -> bool:\n        return (\n            self.max.x >= other.min.x\n            and self.min.x <= other.max.x\n            and self.max.y >= other.min.y\n            and self.min.y <= other.max.y\n        )\n\n    @classmethod\n    def from_center(cls, center: Vec2, half_extents: Vec2) -> \"AABB\":\n        return cls(\n            min=center - half_extents,\n            max=center + half_extents,\n        )\n\n\n@dataclass(slots=True)\nclass RigidBody:\n    \"\"\"\n    Minimal rigid-body representation.\n\n    Only rectangle shapes are supported in this reference implementation, but\n    the component data model can be extended without changing the simulator’s\n    public interface.\n    \"\"\"\n    id: str\n    position: Vec2\n    velocity: Vec2\n    half_extents: Vec2  # half-width/height (meters)\n    mass: float = 1.0\n    restitution: float = 0.5\n    static: bool = False\n    # Non-serialized runtime fields\n    force_acc: Vec2 = field(default_factory=lambda: Vec2(0.0, 0.0), repr=False)\n\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n    # Convenience wrappers\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n    @property\n    def inv_mass(self) -> float:\n        \"\"\"Inverse mass—0 for static or infinite-mass bodies.\"\"\"\n        return 0.0 if self.static else 1.0 / self.mass\n\n    @property\n    def aabb(self) -> AABB:\n        return AABB.from_center(self.position, self.half_extents)\n\n    def apply_force(self, force: Vec2):\n        \"\"\"Accumulate force for the current frame (F = m * a).\"\"\"\n        if not self.static:\n            self.force_acc = self.force_acc + force\n\n\n# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Physics Simulator\n# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nclass PhysicsSimulator:\n    \"\"\"\n    Stateless 2-D physics simulator.\n\n    Usage (inside Lambda):\n    ----------------------\n    def handler(event, context):\n        return PhysicsSimulator.lambda_handler(event, context)\n    \"\"\"\n\n    # Gravity can be overridden at runtime via env-var for gameplay tuning.\n    GRAVITY = Vec2(\n        float(os.getenv(\"LEDGERQUEST_GRAVITY_X\", \"0\")),\n        float(os.getenv(\"LEDGERQUEST_GRAVITY_Y\", \"-9.81\")),\n    )\n\n    # Velocity threshold below which bodies will be considered “sleeping”.\n    SLEEP_TOLERANCE = float(os.getenv(\"LEDGERQUEST_SLEEP_TOLERANCE\", \"0.01\"))\n\n    # Safe-guard to avoid unbounded compute for huge payloads.\n    MAX_BODIES_PER_INVOCATION = int(os.getenv(\"LEDGERQUEST_MAX_BODIES\", \"512\"))\n\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n    # Lambda Entrypoint\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n    @classmethod\n    def lambda_handler(cls, event, _context):\n        \"\"\"\n        AWS Lambda handler. Receives JSON payload:\n        {\n          \"delta_time\": 0.016,\n          \"bodies\": [\n              {\n                  \"id\": \"entity-uuid\",\n                  \"position\": [x, y],\n                  \"velocity\": [vx, vy],\n                  \"half_extents\": [hx, hy],\n                  \"mass\": 1.0,\n                  \"restitution\": 0.5,\n                  \"static\": false\n              },\n              ...\n          ]\n        }\n        \"\"\"\n        try:\n            world_state = cls._deserialize_event(event)\n        except DeserializationError as exc:\n            logger.exception(\"Invalid input payload.\")\n            raise\n\n        logger.debug(\"Simulating %d bodies (dt=%.6f)\",\n                     len(world_state), event[\"delta_time\"])\n\n        cls._validate_payload_size(len(world_state))\n\n        cls._integrate(world_state, event[\"delta_time\"])\n        cls._broad_and_narrow_phase(world_state)\n\n        # Prepare JSON-serialisable response\n        response = {\n            \"bodies\": [\n                {\n                    \"id\": body.id,\n                    \"position\": body.position.to_json(),\n                    \"velocity\": body.velocity.to_json(),\n                }\n                for body in world_state\n            ]\n        }\n        return json.dumps(response)\n\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n    # Public helper for unit tests or in-process calls\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n    @classmethod\n    def step(\n        cls,\n        bodies: List[RigidBody],\n        delta_time: float,\n        *,\n        apply_gravity: bool = True,\n    ) -> List[RigidBody]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Advance simulation by `delta_time` and return *mutated* bodies list.\n\n        The method is kept pure: no network, no I/O, no global state mutation,\n        which makes it deterministic and easy to unit-test.\n        \"\"\"\n        if apply_gravity:\n            for body in bodies:\n                if not body.static:\n                    body.apply_force(cls.GRAVITY * body.mass)\n\n        cls._integrate(bodies, delta_time)\n        cls._broad_and_narrow_phase(bodies)\n        return bodies\n\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n    # Internal – Payload (de)serialization\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n    @staticmethod\n    def _deserialize_event(event: Dict) -> List[RigidBody]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Convert the inbound JSON event into a list of `RigidBody` instances.\n        Raises `DeserializationError` when invalid.\n        \"\"\"\n        try:\n            bodies_payload = event[\"bodies\"]\n            delta_time = float(event[\"delta_time\"])\n            if delta_time <= 0.0 or not math.isfinite(delta_time):\n                raise ValueError(\"delta_time must be positive and finite.\")\n        except (KeyError, TypeError, ValueError) as exc:\n            raise DeserializationError(str(exc)) from exc\n\n        bodies = []\n        for raw in bodies_payload:\n            try:\n                body = RigidBody(\n                    id=raw.get(\"id\") or str(uuid.uuid4()),\n                    position=Vec2.from_iterable(raw[\"position\"]),\n                    velocity=Vec2.from_iterable(raw[\"velocity\"]),\n                    half_extents=Vec2.from_iterable(raw[\"half_extents\"]),\n                    mass=float(raw.get(\"mass\", 1.0)),\n                    restitution=float(raw.get(\"restitution\", 0.5)),\n                    static=bool(raw.get(\"static\", False)),\n                )\n                bodies.append(body)\n            except Exception as exc:\n                raise DeserializationError(\n                    f\"Malformed body entry: {raw}\"\n                ) from exc\n\n        return bodies\n\n    @classmethod\n    def _validate_payload_size(cls, n: int):\n        if n > cls.MAX_BODIES_PER_INVOCATION:\n            raise PhysicsError(\n                f\"Too many bodies for a single invocation \"\n                f\"({n} > {cls.MAX_BODIES_PER_INVOCATION}).\"\n            )\n\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n    # Internal – Integration\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n    @staticmethod\n    def _integrate(bodies: Iterable[RigidBody], dt: float):\n        \"\"\"Semi-implicit Euler integration.\"\"\"\n        for body in bodies:\n            if body.static:\n                continue\n\n            # a = F * inv_mass\n            acceleration = body.force_acc * body.inv_mass\n            body.velocity = body.velocity + acceleration * dt\n            body.position = body.position + body.velocity * dt\n\n            # Clear forces for next frame\n            body.force_acc = Vec2(0.0, 0.0)\n\n            # Sleep optimisation\n            if body.velocity.magnitude() < PhysicsSimulator.SLEEP_TOLERANCE:\n                body.velocity = Vec2(0.0, 0.0)\n\n            # Post-integration invariant check\n            if (\n                not math.isfinite(body.position.x)\n                or not math.isfinite(body.position.y)\n            ):\n                raise SimulationInvariantViolation(\n                    f\"Non-finite position for body {body.id}\"\n                )\n\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n    # Internal – Collision Phases\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n    @staticmethod\n    def _broad_and_narrow_phase(bodies: List[RigidBody]):\n        \"\"\"\n        Perform broad-phase AABB checks followed by narrow-phase impulse\n        resolution for overlapping pairs.\n        \"\"\"\n        # Simple N^2 broadphase due to low `MAX_BODIES_PER_INVOCATION`.\n        for i, a in enumerate(bodies):\n            if a.static:\n                continue\n            for b in bodies[i + 1 :]:\n                # Skip pairs with two static bodies\n                if a.static and b.static:\n                    continue\n\n                if not a.aabb.overlaps(b.aabb):\n                    continue  # broad-phase cull\n\n                PhysicsSimulator._resolve_collision(a, b)\n\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n    # Internal – Narrow-phase Resolution\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n    @staticmethod\n    def _resolve_collision(a: RigidBody, b: RigidBody):\n        \"\"\"\n        Very small subset of SAT—for axis-aligned rectangles only.\n        Calculates penetration depth along each axis and resolves along the\n        axis of least penetration.\n        \"\"\"\n        # Relative vector\n        n = b.position - a.position\n\n        # Overlap on x axis\n        x_overlap = (a.half_extents.x + b.half_extents.x) - abs(n.x)\n        if x_overlap <= 0:\n            return  # no collision\n\n        # Overlap on y axis\n        y_overlap = (a.half_extents.y + b.half_extents.y) - abs(n.y)\n        if y_overlap <= 0:\n            return  # no collision\n\n        # Find axis of least penetration\n        if x_overlap < y_overlap:\n            mtv = Vec2(x_overlap if n.x > 0 else -x_overlap, 0)\n        else:\n            mtv = Vec2(0, y_overlap if n.y > 0 else -y_overlap)\n\n        # Positional correction (minimum translation vector)\n        PhysicsSimulator._positional_correction(a, b, mtv)\n\n        # Relative velocity\n        rv = b.velocity - a.velocity\n        # Normal is direction of MTV\n        normal = mtv.normalize()\n\n        vel_along_normal = rv.dot(normal)\n        if vel_along_normal > 0:\n            return  # bodies separating\n\n        restitution = min(a.restitution, b.restitution)\n\n        # Impulse scalar\n        j = -(1 + restitution) * vel_along_normal\n        inv_mass_sum = a.inv_mass + b.inv_mass\n        if inv_mass_sum == 0:\n            return\n\n        j /= inv_mass_sum\n\n        # Apply impulse\n        impulse = normal * j\n        if not a.static:\n            a.velocity = a.velocity - impulse * a.inv_mass\n        if not b.static:\n            b.velocity = b.velocity + impulse * b.inv_mass\n\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n    # Internal – Positional Correction\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n    @staticmethod\n    def _positional_correction(a: RigidBody, b: RigidBody, mtv: Vec2):\n        \"\"\"\n        Positional correction to avoid sinking due to numerical errors.\n        \"\"\"\n        percent = 0.8  # higher percentage = more correction\n        slop = 0.01    # penetration allowance\n        correction_magnitude = max(mtv.magnitude() - slop, 0.0) * percent\n        correction = mtv.normalize() * correction_magnitude\n\n        total_inv_mass = a.inv_mass + b.inv_mass\n        if total_inv_mass == 0:\n            return\n\n        if not a.static:\n            a.position = a.position - correction * (a.inv_mass / total_inv_mass)\n        if not b.static:\n            b.position = b.position + correction * (b.inv_mass / total_inv_mass)\n\n\n# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Module test hook (executed only when run locally)\n# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    # Quick sanity test – two boxes falling onto the floor.\n    ground = RigidBody(\n        id=\"ground\",\n        position=Vec2(0.0, -1.0),\n        velocity=Vec2(0.0, 0.0),\n        half_extents=Vec2(5.0, 0.5),\n        static=True,\n    )\n\n    box = RigidBody(\n        id=\"box\",\n        position=Vec2(0.0, 2.0),\n        velocity=Vec2(0.0, 0.0),\n        half_extents=Vec2(0.5, 0.5),\n        mass=1.0,\n        restitution=0.3,\n    )\n\n    bodies = [ground, box]\n    dt = 1.0 / 60.0\n\n    for frame in range(240):  # 4 seconds\n        PhysicsSimulator.step(bodies, dt)\n        logger.info(\n            \"Frame %03d | pos=%.3f, %.3f vel=%.3f, %.3f\",\n            frame,\n            box.position.x,\n            box.position.y,\n            box.velocity.x,\n            box.velocity.y,\n        )\n```", "numLines": 505, "startLine": 1, "totalLines": 505}}
